revision.text:
{{日本の古墳| 名称=長岡百穴古墳| 所在地=栃木県宇都宮市長岡町| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|35|35|N|139|53|3|E|}}| 形状=横穴墓群| 規模=東群44基、西群8基、計52基| 築造年代=7世紀前期頃| 被葬者=| 史跡指定=県指定史跡（長岡百穴古墳）| 出土品=| 画像=}}
'''長岡百穴古墳'''（ながおかひゃくあなこふん）は、[[栃木県]][[宇都宮市]]長岡町に存在する[[横穴墓]]群である。'''長岡百穴'''と略称されることが多い。
== 概要 == 長岡百穴古墳は7世紀前期頃に造成された家族墓的な要素が強い横穴墓群と考えられている。東群44基と西群8基の計52基の横穴（縦横各1メートル、奥行き約2メートル程度）が南面する[[軽石]][[凝灰岩]]の傾斜面に開口している。近代では既に横穴が露出して原型を留めておらず、埋葬品に基づく考証は不能であるが、床面に側溝を有する穴も散見されるなど、比較的成熟した技術をもって掘削された墓穴とみられることから、[[古墳時代]]末期のものと推定されている。ほぼすべての穴の壁面に[[室町時代]]から[[江戸時代]]にかけて彫られたとみられる[[地蔵菩薩]]像や[[馬頭観音]]像が見られることから、外気に曝されていた期間が非常に長いものと窺われ、その為か欠損も多く保存状態はあまり良くない。宇都宮に伝わる「[[百々目鬼#百目鬼伝説|百目鬼伝説]]」にも登場することから、本伝説が成立した頃には既に百穴古墳の存在が知られていたものと推察される。
== 周辺環境 == 長岡百穴古墳は[[宇都宮市]]北部の[[宇都宮丘陵]]の南部、[[戸祭山]]の北西麓で[[田川 (利根川水系)|田川]]と[[釜川]]に挟まれた台地上に立地する。周辺には当墳をはじめ、[[谷口山古墳]]や[[北山古墳群]]、[[瓦塚古墳群]]、[[戸祭大塚古墳]]などの古代古墳群、また[[奈良時代]]の遺跡である[[水道山瓦窯跡]]などの古代遺跡が数多く認められており、[[豊郷まほろばの道]]が整備されている。一方、近年では[[宇都宮環状道路]]が近隣地に開通し、宅地化が進んでいる。
== アクセス == ;路線バス * [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[宇都宮駅]]西口から ** [[関東自動車 (栃木県)|関東バス]] 帝京大学・宇都宮美術館線に乗車約15分、「豊郷台入口」下車徒歩20分。
;車 * [[国道119号]]（[[宇都宮環状道路|豊郷バイパス]]）長岡町交差点から ** [[栃木県道157号下岡本上戸祭線|長岡街道]]を北進、3分。 * [[栃木県道63号藤原宇都宮線|田原街道]]関堀町北交差点から ** 長岡街道を西進、3分。
== 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧]] * [[栃木県指定文化財一覧]] * [[北山古墳群]] * [[瓦塚古墳群]] * [[谷口山古墳]] * [[戸祭大塚古墳]] * [[豊郷まほろばの道]] * [[百々目鬼#百目鬼伝説|百目鬼伝説]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:なかおかひやくあなこふん}} [[Category:栃木県の歴史]] [[Category:栃木県の古墳]] [[Category:宇都宮市]]

{{日本の古墳| 名称=長岡百穴古墳| 所在地=栃木県宇都宮市長岡町| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|35|35|N|139|53|3|E|}}| 形状=横穴墓群| 規模=東群44基、西群8基、計52基| 築造年代=7世紀前期頃| 被葬者=| 史跡指定=県指定史跡（長岡百穴古墳）| 出土品=| 画像=}}

== アクセス == ;路線バス * [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[宇都宮駅]]西口から ** [[関東自動車 (栃木県)|関東バス]] 帝京大学・宇都宮美術館線に乗車約15分、「豊郷台入口」下車徒歩20分。
;車 * [[国道119号]]（[[宇都宮環状道路|豊郷バイパス]]）長岡町交差点から ** [[栃木県道157号下岡本上戸祭線|長岡街道]]を北進、3分。 * [[栃木県道63号藤原宇都宮線|田原街道]]関堀町北交差点から ** 長岡街道を西進、3分。

revision.text:
{{日本の古墳| |名称=長岡百穴古墳 |画像=[[ファイル:Nagaoka hyakuana east01.jpg|300px]] |所在地=栃木県宇都宮市長岡町 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|35|35|N|139|53|3|E|}} |形状=横穴墓群 |規模=東群44基、西群8基、計52基 |築造年代=7世紀前期頃 |被葬者= |史跡指定=県指定史跡（長岡百穴古墳） |出土品= }}
'''長岡百穴古墳'''（ながおかひゃくあなこふん）は、[[栃木県]][[宇都宮市]]長岡町に存在する[[横穴墓]]群である。'''長岡百穴'''と略称されることが多い。
== 概要 == 長岡百穴古墳は7世紀前期頃に造成された家族墓的な要素が強い横穴墓群と考えられている。東群44基と西群8基の計52基の横穴（縦横各1メートル、奥行き約2メートル程度）が南面する[[軽石]][[凝灰岩]]の傾斜面に開口している。近代では既に横穴が露出して原型を留めておらず、埋葬品に基づく考証は不能であるが、床面に側溝を有する穴も散見されるなど、比較的成熟した技術をもって掘削された墓穴とみられることから、[[古墳時代]]末期のものと推定されている。ほぼすべての穴の壁面に[[室町時代]]から[[江戸時代]]にかけて彫られたとみられる[[地蔵菩薩]]像や[[馬頭観音]]像が見られることから、外気に曝されていた期間が非常に長いものと窺われ、その為か欠損も多く保存状態はあまり良くない。宇都宮に伝わる「[[百々目鬼#百目鬼伝説|百目鬼伝説]]」にも登場することから、本伝説が成立した頃には既に百穴古墳の存在が知られていたものと推察される。
<gallery> ファイル:Nagaoka hyakuana east02.jpg|東群を西側から ファイル:Nagaoka hyakuana west.jpg|西群 ファイル:Nagaoka hyakuana inside.jpg|内部 ファイル:Nagaoka hyakuana monument.jpg|[[小杉放庵]]の歌碑 </gallery>
== 周辺環境 == 長岡百穴古墳は[[宇都宮市]]北部の[[宇都宮丘陵]]の南部、[[戸祭山]]の北西麓で[[田川 (利根川水系)|田川]]と[[釜川]]に挟まれた台地上に立地する。周辺には当墳をはじめ、[[谷口山古墳]]や[[北山古墳群]]、[[瓦塚古墳群]]、[[戸祭大塚古墳]]などの古代古墳群、また[[奈良時代]]の遺跡である[[水道山瓦窯跡]]などの古代遺跡が数多く認められており、[[豊郷まほろばの道]]が整備されている。一方、近年では[[宇都宮環状道路]]が近隣地に開通し、宅地化が進んでいる。
== 参考文献 == * 読売新聞栃木版 2016年5月8日 27面掲載。
== アクセス == ;路線バス * [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[宇都宮駅]]西口から ** [[関東自動車 (栃木県)|関東バス]] 帝京大学・宇都宮美術館線に乗車約15分、「豊郷台入口」下車徒歩20分。
;車 * [[国道119号]]（[[宇都宮環状道路|豊郷バイパス]]）長岡町交差点から ** [[栃木県道157号下岡本上戸祭線|長岡街道]]を北進、3分。 * [[栃木県道63号藤原宇都宮線|田原街道]]関堀町北交差点から ** 長岡街道を西進、3分。
== 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧]] * [[栃木県指定文化財一覧]] * [[北山古墳群]] * [[瓦塚古墳群]] * [[谷口山古墳]] * [[戸祭大塚古墳]] * [[豊郷まほろばの道]] * [[百々目鬼#百目鬼伝説|百目鬼伝説]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:なかおかひやくあなこふん}} [[Category:栃木県の古墳]] [[Category:横穴墓]] [[Category:宇都宮市の歴史]] [[Category:栃木県指定史跡]]

{{日本の古墳| |名称=長岡百穴古墳 |画像=[[ファイル:Nagaoka hyakuana east01.jpg|300px]] |所在地=栃木県宇都宮市長岡町 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|35|35|N|139|53|3|E|}} |形状=横穴墓群 |規模=東群44基、西群8基、計52基 |築造年代=7世紀前期頃 |被葬者= |史跡指定=県指定史跡（長岡百穴古墳） |出土品= }}
'''長岡百穴古墳'''（ながおかひゃくあなこふん）は、[[栃木県]][[宇都宮市]]長岡町に存在する[[横穴墓]]群である。'''長岡百穴'''と略称されることが多い。
== 概要 == 長岡百穴古墳は7世紀前期頃に造成された家族墓的な要素が強い横穴墓群と考えられている。東群44基と西群8基の計52基の横穴（縦横各1メートル、奥行き約2メートル程度）が南面する[[軽石]][[凝灰岩]]の傾斜面に開口している。近代では既に横穴が露出して原型を留めておらず、埋葬品に基づく考証は不能であるが、床面に側溝を有する穴も散見されるなど、比較的成熟した技術をもって掘削された墓穴とみられることから、[[古墳時代]]末期のものと推定されている。ほぼすべての穴の壁面に[[室町時代]]から[[江戸時代]]にかけて彫られたとみられる[[地蔵菩薩]]像や[[馬頭観音]]像が見られることから、外気に曝されていた期間が非常に長いものと窺われ、その為か欠損も多く保存状態はあまり良くない。宇都宮に伝わる「[[百目鬼|百目鬼伝説]]」にも登場することから、本伝説が成立した頃には既に百穴古墳の存在が知られていたものと推察される。
<gallery> ファイル:Nagaoka hyakuana east02.jpg|東群を西側から ファイル:Nagaoka hyakuana west.jpg|西群 ファイル:Nagaoka hyakuana inside.jpg|内部 ファイル:Nagaoka hyakuana monument.jpg|[[小杉放庵]]の歌碑 </gallery>
== 周辺環境 == 長岡百穴古墳は[[宇都宮市]]北部の[[宇都宮丘陵]]の南部、[[戸祭山]]の北西麓で[[田川 (利根川水系)|田川]]と[[釜川]]に挟まれた台地上に立地する。周辺には当墳をはじめ、[[谷口山古墳]]や[[北山古墳群]]、[[瓦塚古墳群]]、[[戸祭大塚古墳]]などの古代古墳群、また[[奈良時代]]の遺跡である[[水道山瓦窯跡]]などの古代遺跡が数多く認められており、[[豊郷まほろばの道]]が整備されている。一方、近年では[[宇都宮環状道路]]が近隣地に開通し、宅地化が進んでいる。
== 参考文献 == * 読売新聞栃木版 2016年5月8日 27面掲載。
== アクセス == ;路線バス * [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[宇都宮駅]]西口から ** [[関東自動車 (栃木県)|関東バス]] 帝京大学・宇都宮美術館線に乗車約15分、「[[豊郷台]]入口」下車徒歩20分。
;車 * [[国道119号]]（[[宇都宮環状道路|豊郷バイパス]]）長岡町交差点から ** [[栃木県道157号下岡本上戸祭線|長岡街道]]を北進、3分。 * [[栃木県道63号藤原宇都宮線|田原街道]]関堀町北交差点から ** 長岡街道を西進、3分。
== 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧]] * [[栃木県指定文化財一覧]] * [[北山古墳群]] * [[瓦塚古墳群]] * [[谷口山古墳]] * [[戸祭大塚古墳]] * [[豊郷まほろばの道]] * [[百目鬼]] * [[うつのみや百景]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:なかおかひやくあなこふん}} [[Category:栃木県の古墳]] [[Category:横穴墓]] [[Category:栃木県指定史跡]] [[Category:宇都宮市の歴史]] [[Category:うつのみや百景]]

revision.text:
{{Japanese Ancient Tombs | Name = Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun | Image = [[File:Nagaoka Hyakuana, higashi-gun-2.jpg|280px]]
Eastern Cluster
[[File:Nagaoka Hyakuana, nishi-gun.jpg|280px]]
Western Cluster | Location = {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Nagaokachō | Coordinates = {{Coord|36|35|35|N|139|53|3|E}} | Shape = Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster | Dimensions = 52 tombs (44 in the Eastern Cluster, 8 in the Western Cluster) | Construction Era = Early 7th century | Buried Figure = | Cultural Heritage Designation = Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun) | Artifacts = }} '''Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun''' (長岡百穴古墳, also known as Nagaoka Hyakuana or the Nagaoka Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster) is a cluster of {{ill|rock-cut tombs|ja|横穴墓}} located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is designated as a historic site by the Tochigi Prefecture. == Overview == The Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun is considered a family-oriented burial site, with tombs believed to have been constructed in the early 7th century. The cluster comprises 52 tombs, with 44 in the Eastern Cluster and 8 in the Western Cluster. Each tomb is approximately 1 meter in height and width and 2 meters in depth, carved into the sloped pumice tuff bedrock facing south. By modern times, the tombs were already exposed, with their original form lost. Artifacts that could have been buried with the deceased are no longer present, making detailed research challenging. However, the tomb designs, including some with side channels on the floors, indicate advanced craftsmanship characteristic of the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Many of the tomb walls feature carvings of {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩}} and {{ill|Bato Kannon|ja|馬頭観音}}, believed to date back to the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} through the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. This suggests prolonged exposure to the elements and considerable cultural use, as evidenced by the inclusion of the site in local folklore, such as the {{ill|Hyakumoku Densetsu (One Hundred Eyes Legend)|ja|百目鬼伝説}}. The site is located on a plateau flanked by the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川}} and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川}}. The surrounding area features several other tumuli and ancient sites, including the {{ill|Taniguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, as well as remains from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. Urbanization has increased in the surrounding area with the development of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}. == Cultural Heritage == === Tochigi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Sites ** Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun - Designated on July 26, 1955. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yomiuri Shimbun, Tochigi Edition, May 8, 2016, p. 27. == Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR West Exit): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} to the Teikyo University/Utsunomiya Museum of Art line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Toyosatodai Entrance" and walk for 20 minutes. ; By Car * From National Route 119 (Toyosato Bypass): ** Proceed north from the Nagaokamachi Intersection along the {{ill|Nagaoka Road|ja|長岡街道}} for 3 minutes. * From {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Route 63|ja|栃木県道63号藤原宇都宮線}}: ** Head west from the Sekibori-cho North Intersection via Nagaoka Road for 3 minutes. == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Nagaoka Hyakuana}} * {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}} * {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}} * {{ill|Taniguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}} * {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}} * {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} * {{ill|Hyakumoku|ja|百目鬼}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun}} [[Category:Rock-cut tombs]] [[Category:Ancient tombs in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Utsunomiya]] [[Category:Tochigi Prefecture-designated historic sites]] [[Category:Utsunomiya Scenic Spots]] [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=]] [[Category:Kk]] [[Category:Redlink Kofun]] [[ja:%E9%95%B7%E5%B2%A1%E7%99%BE%E7%A9%B4%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]][[ja:&action=edit]]

{{Japanese Ancient Tombs | Name = Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun | image = [[File:Nagaoka Hyakuana, higashi-gun-2.jpg|280px]]
Eastern Cluster
[[File:Nagaoka Hyakuana, nishi-gun.jpg|280px]]
Western Cluster | Location = {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Nagaokachō | Coordinates = {{Coord|36|35|35|N|139|53|3|E}} | Shape = Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster | Dimensions = 52 tombs (44 in the Eastern Cluster, 8 in the Western Cluster) | Construction Era = Early 7th century | Buried Figure = | Cultural Heritage Designation = Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun) | Artifacts = }} '''Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun''' (長岡百穴古墳, also known as Nagaoka Hyakuana or the Nagaoka Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster) is a cluster of {{ill|rock-cut tombs|ja|横穴墓}} located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is designated as a historic site by the Tochigi Prefecture. == Overview == The Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun is considered a family-oriented burial site, with tombs believed to have been constructed in the early 7th century. The cluster comprises 52 tombs, with 44 in the Eastern Cluster and 8 in the Western Cluster. Each tomb is approximately 1 meter in height and width and 2 meters in depth, carved into the sloped pumice tuff bedrock facing south. By modern times, the tombs were already exposed, with their original form lost. Artifacts that could have been buried with the deceased are no longer present, making detailed research challenging. However, the tomb designs, including some with side channels on the floors, indicate advanced craftsmanship characteristic of the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Many of the tomb walls feature carvings of {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩}} and {{ill|Bato Kannon|ja|馬頭観音}}, believed to date back to the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} through the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. This suggests prolonged exposure to the elements and considerable cultural use, as evidenced by the inclusion of the site in local folklore, such as the {{ill|Hyakumoku Densetsu (One Hundred Eyes Legend)|ja|百目鬼伝説}}. File:Nagaoka hyakuana inside.jpg|Interior view File:Nagaoka hyakuana monument.jpg|Poetic inscription by {{ill|Kosugi Hōan|ja|小杉放庵}} == Surrounding Environment == The Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun is situated in the northern part of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, at the southwestern foothills of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}, near {{ill|Tomatsuriyama|ja|戸祭山}}. The site is located on a plateau flanked by the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川}} and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川}}. The surrounding area features several other tumuli and ancient sites, including the {{ill|Taniguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, as well as remains from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. These sites are linked by the {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}}. The tombs are surrounded by greenery, including {{ill|Quercus serrata|ja|コナラ}} and {{ill|Quercus acutissima|ja|クヌギ}}. The area is designated as the Nagaoka Green Conservation Area by Tochigi Prefecture{{Cite web |url=https://www.pref.tochigi.lg.jp/d04/30jikannti/10nagaoka.html|title=10 Nagaoka Green Conservation Area|website=Tochigi Prefecture |accessdate=2024-11-11}}. Urbanization has increased in the surrounding area with the development of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}. == Cultural Heritage == === Tochigi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Sites ** Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun - Designated on July 26, 1955. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yomiuri Shimbun, Tochigi Edition, May 8, 2016, p. 27. == Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR West Exit): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} to the Teikyo University/Utsunomiya Museum of Art line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Toyosatodai Entrance" and walk for 20 minutes. ; By Car * From National Route 119 (Toyosato Bypass): ** Proceed north from the Nagaokamachi Intersection along the {{ill|Nagaoka Road|ja|長岡街道}} for 3 minutes. * From {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Route 63|ja|栃木県道63号藤原宇都宮線}}: ** Head west from the Sekibori-cho North Intersection via Nagaoka Road for 3 minutes. == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Nagaoka Hyakuana}} * {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}} * {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}} * {{ill|Taniguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}} * {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}} * {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} * {{ill|Hyakumoku|ja|百目鬼}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun}} [[Category:Rock-cut tombs]] [[Category:Ancient tombs in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Utsunomiya]] [[Category:Tochigi Prefecture-designated historic sites]] [[Category:Utsunomiya Scenic Spots]] [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=]] [[Category:Kk]] [[Category:Redlink Kofun]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:01-17]][[ja:%E9%95%B7%E5%B2%A1%E7%99%BE%E7%A9%B4%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:01-17]][[ja:&action=edit]]

Alight at "Toyosatodai Entrance" and walk for 20 minutes. ; By Car * From National Route 119 (Toyosato Bypass): ** Proceed north from the Nagaokamachi Intersection along the {{ill|Nagaoka Road|ja|長岡街道}} for 3 minutes. * From {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Route 63|ja|栃木県道63号藤原宇都宮線}}: ** Head west from the Sekibori-cho North Intersection via Nagaoka Road for 3 minutes. == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Nagaoka Hyakuana}} * {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}} * {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}} * {{ill|Taniguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}} * {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}} * {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} * {{ill|Hyakumoku|ja|百目鬼}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun}} [[Category:Rock-cut tombs]] [[Category:Ancient tombs in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Utsunomiya]] [[Category:Tochigi Prefecture-designated historic sites]] [[Category:Utsunomiya Scenic Spots]] [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=]] [[Category:Kk]] [[Category:Redlink Kofun]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:01-17]][[ja:%E9%95%B7%E5%B2%A1%E7%99%BE%E7%A9%B4%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]][[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:01-17]][[ja:&action=edit]]

{{Infobox ancient sites | name = Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun | image = [[File:Nagaoka Hyakuana, higashi-gun-2.jpg|280px]]
Eastern Cluster
[[File:Nagaoka Hyakuana, nishi-gun.jpg|280px]]
Western Cluster | location = {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Nagaokachō | Coordinates = {{Coord|36|35|35|N|139|53|3|E}} | Shape = Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster | Dimensions = 52 tombs (44 in the Eastern Cluster, 8 in the Western Cluster) | Construction Era = Early 7th century | Buried Figure = | Cultural Heritage Designation = Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun) | Artifacts = }} '''Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun''' (長岡百穴古墳, also known as Nagaoka Hyakuana or the Nagaoka Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster) is a cluster of {{ill|rock-cut tombs|ja|横穴墓}} located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is designated as a historic site by the Tochigi Prefecture. == Overview == The Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun is considered a family-oriented burial site, with tombs believed to have been constructed in the early 7th century. The cluster comprises 52 tombs, with 44 in the Eastern Cluster and 8 in the Western Cluster. Each tomb is approximately 1 meter in height and width and 2 meters in depth, carved into the sloped pumice tuff bedrock facing south. By modern times, the tombs were already exposed, with their original form lost. Artifacts that could have been buried with the deceased are no longer present, making detailed research challenging. However, the tomb designs, including some with side channels on the floors, indicate advanced craftsmanship characteristic of the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Many of the tomb walls feature carvings of {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩}} and {{ill|Bato Kannon|ja|馬頭観音}}, believed to date back to the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} through the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. This suggests prolonged exposure to the elements and considerable cultural use, as evidenced by the inclusion of the site in local folklore, such as the {{ill|Hyakumoku Densetsu (One Hundred Eyes Legend)|ja|百目鬼伝説}}. File:Nagaoka hyakuana inside.jpg|Interior view File:Nagaoka hyakuana monument.jpg|Poetic inscription by {{ill|Kosugi Hōan|ja|小杉放庵}} == Surrounding Environment == The Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun is situated in the northern part of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, at the southwestern foothills of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}, near {{ill|Tomatsuriyama|ja|戸祭山}}. The site is located on a plateau flanked by the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川}} and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川}}. The surrounding area features several other tumuli and ancient sites, including the {{ill|Taniguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, as well as remains from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. These sites are linked by the {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}}. The tombs are surrounded by greenery, including {{ill|Quercus serrata|ja|コナラ}} and {{ill|Quercus acutissima|ja|クヌギ}}. The area is designated as the Nagaoka Green Conservation Area by Tochigi Prefecture{{Cite web |url=https://www.pref.tochigi.lg.jp/d04/30jikannti/10nagaoka.html|title=10 Nagaoka Green Conservation Area|website=Tochigi Prefecture |accessdate=2024-11-11}}. Urbanization has increased in the surrounding area with the development of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}. == Cultural Heritage == === Tochigi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Sites ** Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun - Designated on July 26, 1955. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yomiuri Shimbun, Tochigi Edition, May 8, 2016, p. 27. == Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR West Exit): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} to the Teikyo University/Utsunomiya Museum of Art line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Toyosatodai Entrance" and walk for 20 minutes. ; By Car * From National Route 119 (Toyosato Bypass): ** Proceed north from the Nagaokamachi Intersection along the {{ill|Nagaoka Road|ja|長岡街道}} for 3 minutes. * From {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Route 63|ja|栃木県道63号藤原宇都宮線}}: ** Head west from the Sekibori-cho North Intersection via Nagaoka Road for 3 minutes. == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Nagaoka Hyakuana}} * {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}} * {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}} * {{ill|Taniguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}} * {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}} * {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} * {{ill|Hyakumoku|ja|百目鬼}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun}} [[Category:Rock-cut tombs]] [[Category:Ancient tombs in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Utsunomiya]] [[Category:Tochigi Prefecture-designated historic sites]] [[Category:Utsunomiya Scenic Spots]] [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=]] [[Category:Kk]] [[Category:Redlink Kofun]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:01-17]][[ja:%E9%95%B7%E5%B2%A1%E7%99%BE%E7%A9%B4%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:01-17]][[ja:&action=edit]]

Alight at "Toyosatodai Entrance" and walk for 20 minutes. ; By Car * From National Route 119 (Toyosato Bypass): ** Proceed north from the Nagaokamachi Intersection along the {{ill|Nagaoka Road|ja|長岡街道}} for 3 minutes. * From {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Route 63|ja|栃木県道63号藤原宇都宮線}}: ** Head west from the Sekibori-cho North Intersection via Nagaoka Road for 3 minutes. == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Nagaoka Hyakuana}} * {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}} * {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}} * {{ill|Taniguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}} * {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}} * {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} * {{ill|Hyakumoku|ja|百目鬼}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun}} [[Category:Rock-cut tombs]] [[Category:Ancient tombs in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Utsunomiya]] [[Category:Tochigi Prefecture-designated historic sites]] [[Category:Utsunomiya Scenic Spots]] [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=]] [[Category:Redlink Kofun]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:01-17]][[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]][[ja:%E9%95%B7%E5%B2%A1%E7%99%BE%E7%A9%B4%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:01-17]][[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]][[ja:&action=edit]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun | image = [[File:Nagaoka Hyakuana, higashi-gun-2.jpg|280px]]
Eastern Cluster
[[File:Nagaoka Hyakuana, nishi-gun.jpg|280px]]
Western Cluster | location = {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Nagaokachō | Coordinates = {{Coord|36|35|35|N|139|53|3|E}} | Shape = Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster | Dimensions = 52 tombs (44 in the Eastern Cluster, 8 in the Western Cluster) | Construction Era = Early 7th century | Buried Figure = | Cultural Heritage Designation = Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun) | Artifacts = }} '''Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun''' (長岡百穴古墳, also known as Nagaoka Hyakuana or the Nagaoka Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster) is a cluster of {{ill|rock-cut tombs|ja|横穴墓}} located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is designated as a historic site by the Tochigi Prefecture. == Overview == The Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun is considered a family-oriented burial site, with tombs believed to have been constructed in the early 7th century. The cluster comprises 52 tombs, with 44 in the Eastern Cluster and 8 in the Western Cluster. Each tomb is approximately 1 meter in height and width and 2 meters in depth, carved into the sloped pumice tuff bedrock facing south. By modern times, the tombs were already exposed, with their original form lost. Artifacts that could have been buried with the deceased are no longer present, making detailed research challenging. However, the tomb designs, including some with side channels on the floors, indicate advanced craftsmanship characteristic of the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Many of the tomb walls feature carvings of {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩}} and {{ill|Hayagriva (Buddhism)|lt=Bato Kannon|ja|馬頭観音}}, believed to date back to the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} through the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. This suggests prolonged exposure to the elements and considerable cultural use, as evidenced by the inclusion of the site in local folklore, such as the {{ill|Hyakumoku Densetsu (One Hundred Eyes Legend)|ja|百目鬼伝説}}. File:Nagaoka hyakuana inside.jpg|Interior view File:Nagaoka hyakuana monument.jpg|Poetic inscription by {{ill|Kosugi Hōan|ja|小杉放庵}} == Surrounding Environment == The Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun is situated in the northern part of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, at the southwestern foothills of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}, near {{ill|Tomatsuriyama|ja|戸祭山}}. The site is located on a plateau flanked by the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川}} and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川}}. The surrounding area features several other tumuli and ancient sites, including the {{ill|Taniguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, as well as remains from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. These sites are linked by the {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}}. The tombs are surrounded by greenery, including {{ill|Quercus serrata|ja|コナラ}} and {{ill|Quercus acutissima|ja|クヌギ}}. The area is designated as the Nagaoka Green Conservation Area by Tochigi Prefecture{{Cite web |url=https://www.pref.tochigi.lg.jp/d04/30jikannti/10nagaoka.html|title=10 Nagaoka Green Conservation Area|website=Tochigi Prefecture |accessdate=2024-11-11}}. Urbanization has increased in the surrounding area with the development of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}. == Cultural Heritage == === Tochigi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Sites ** Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun - Designated on July 26, 1955. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yomiuri Shimbun, Tochigi Edition, May 8, 2016, p. 27. == Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR West Exit): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} to the Teikyo University/Utsunomiya Museum of Art line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Toyosatodai Entrance" and walk for 20 minutes. ; By Car * From National Route 119 (Toyosato Bypass): ** Proceed north from the Nagaokamachi Intersection along the {{ill|Nagaoka Road|ja|長岡街道}} for 3 minutes. * From {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Route 63|ja|栃木県道63号藤原宇都宮線}}: ** Head west from the Sekibori-cho North Intersection via Nagaoka Road for 3 minutes. == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Nagaoka Hyakuana}} * {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}} * {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}} * {{ill|Taniguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}} * {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}} * {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} * {{ill|Hyakumoku|ja|百目鬼}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun}} [[Category:Rock-cut tombs]] [[Category:Ancient tombs in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Utsunomiya]] [[Category:Tochigi Prefecture-designated historic sites]] [[Category:Utsunomiya Scenic Spots]] [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=]] [[Category:Redlink Kofun]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:01-17]][[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]][[ja:%E9%95%B7%E5%B2%A1%E7%99%BE%E7%A9%B4%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:01-17]][[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]][[ja:&action=edit]]

Alight at "Toyosatodai Entrance" and walk for 20 minutes. ; By Car * From National Route 119 (Toyosato Bypass): ** Proceed north from the Nagaokamachi Intersection along the {{ill|Nagaoka Road|ja|長岡街道}} for 3 minutes. * From {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Route 63|ja|栃木県道63号藤原宇都宮線}}: ** Head west from the Sekibori-cho North Intersection via Nagaoka Road for 3 minutes. == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Nagaoka Hyakuana}} * {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}} * {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}} * {{ill|Taniguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}} * {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}} * {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} * {{ill|Hyakumoku|ja|百目鬼}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun}} [[Category:Rock-cut tombs]] [[Category:Ancient tombs in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Utsunomiya]] [[Category:Tochigi Prefecture-designated historic sites]] [[Category:Utsunomiya Scenic Spots]] [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=]] [[Category:Link complete]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]][[ja:%E9%95%B7%E5%B2%A1%E7%99%BE%E7%A9%B4%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]][[ja:&action=edit]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun | image = [[File:Nagaoka Hyakuana, higashi-gun-2.jpg|280px]]
Eastern Cluster
[[File:Nagaoka Hyakuana, nishi-gun.jpg|280px]]
Western Cluster | location = {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Nagaokachō | Coordinates = {{Coord|36|35|35|N|139|53|3|E}} | Shape = Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster | Dimensions = 52 tombs (44 in the Eastern Cluster, 8 in the Western Cluster) | Construction Era = Early 7th century | Buried Figure = | Cultural Heritage Designation = Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun) | Artifacts = }} '''Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun''' (長岡百穴古墳, also known as Nagaoka Hyakuana or the Nagaoka Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster) is a cluster of {{ill|rock-cut tombs|ja|横穴墓}} located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is designated as a historic site by the Tochigi Prefecture. == Overview == The Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun is considered a family-oriented burial site, with tombs believed to have been constructed in the early 7th century. The cluster comprises 52 tombs, with 44 in the Eastern Cluster and 8 in the Western Cluster. Each tomb is approximately 1 meter in height and width and 2 meters in depth, carved into the sloped pumice tuff bedrock facing south. By modern times, the tombs were already exposed, with their original form lost. Artifacts that could have been buried with the deceased are no longer present, making detailed research challenging. However, the tomb designs, including some with side channels on the floors, indicate advanced craftsmanship characteristic of the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Many of the tomb walls feature carvings of {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩}} and {{ill|Hayagriva (Buddhism)|lt=Bato Kannon|ja|馬頭観音}}, believed to date back to the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} through the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. This suggests prolonged exposure to the elements and considerable cultural use, as evidenced by the inclusion of the site in local folklore, such as the {{ill|Hyakumoku Densetsu (One Hundred Eyes Legend)|ja|百目鬼伝説}}. File:Nagaoka hyakuana inside.jpg|Interior view File:Nagaoka hyakuana monument.jpg|Poetic inscription by {{ill|Misai Kosugi|ja|小杉放庵}} == Surrounding Environment == The Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun is situated in the northern part of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, at the southwestern foothills of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}, near {{ill|Tomatsuriyama|ja|戸祭山}}. The site is located on a plateau flanked by the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川}} and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川}}. The surrounding area features several other tumuli and ancient sites, including the {{ill|Taniguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, as well as remains from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. These sites are linked by the {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}}. The tombs are surrounded by greenery, including {{ill|Quercus serrata|ja|コナラ}} and {{ill|Quercus acutissima|ja|クヌギ}}. The area is designated as the Nagaoka Green Conservation Area by Tochigi Prefecture{{Cite web |url=https://www.pref.tochigi.lg.jp/d04/30jikannti/10nagaoka.html|title=10 Nagaoka Green Conservation Area|website=Tochigi Prefecture |accessdate=2024-11-11}}. Urbanization has increased in the surrounding area with the development of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}. == Cultural Heritage == === Tochigi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Sites ** Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun - Designated on July 26, 1955. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yomiuri Shimbun, Tochigi Edition, May 8, 2016, p. 27. == Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR West Exit): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} to the Teikyo University/Utsunomiya Museum of Art line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Toyosatodai Entrance" and walk for 20 minutes. ; By Car * From National Route 119 (Toyosato Bypass): ** Proceed north from the Nagaokamachi Intersection along the {{ill|Nagaoka Road|ja|長岡街道}} for 3 minutes. * From {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Route 63|ja|栃木県道63号藤原宇都宮線}}: ** Head west from the Sekibori-cho North Intersection via Nagaoka Road for 3 minutes. == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Nagaoka Hyakuana}} * {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}} * {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}} * {{ill|Taniguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}} * {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}} * {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} * {{ill|Hyakumoku|ja|百目鬼}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun}} [[Category:Rock-cut tombs]] [[Category:Ancient tombs in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Utsunomiya]] [[Category:Tochigi Prefecture-designated historic sites]] [[Category:Utsunomiya Scenic Spots]] [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=]] [[Category:Link complete]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]][[ja:%E9%95%B7%E5%B2%A1%E7%99%BE%E7%A9%B4%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]][[ja:&action=edit]]

Alight at "Toyosatodai Entrance" and walk for 20 minutes. ; By Car * From National Route 119 (Toyosato Bypass): ** Proceed north from the Nagaokamachi Intersection along the {{ill|Nagaoka Road|ja|長岡街道}} for 3 minutes. * From {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Route 63|ja|栃木県道63号藤原宇都宮線}}: ** Head west from the Sekibori-cho North Intersection via Nagaoka Road for 3 minutes. == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Nagaoka Hyakuana}} * {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}} * {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}} * {{ill|Taniguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}} * {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}} * {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} * {{ill|Hyakumoku|ja|百目鬼}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun}} [[Category:Rock-cut tombs]] [[Category:Ancient tombs in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Utsunomiya]] [[Category:Tochigi Prefecture-designated historic sites]] [[Category:Utsunomiya Scenic Spots]] [[Category:Link complete]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]][[ja:%E9%95%B7%E5%B2%A1%E7%99%BE%E7%A9%B4%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:&action=edit]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun | image = [[File:Nagaoka Hyakuana, higashi-gun-2.jpg|280px]]
Eastern Cluster
[[File:Nagaoka Hyakuana, nishi-gun.jpg|280px]]
Western Cluster | location = {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Nagaokachō | Coordinates = {{Coord|36|35|35|N|139|53|3|E}} | Shape = Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster | Dimensions = 52 tombs (44 in the Eastern Cluster, 8 in the Western Cluster) | Construction Era = Early 7th century | Buried Figure = | Cultural Heritage Designation = Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun) | Artifacts = }} '''Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun''' (長岡百穴古墳, also known as Nagaoka Hyakuana or the Nagaoka Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster) is a cluster of {{ill|rock-cut tombs|ja|横穴墓}} located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is designated as a historic site by the Tochigi Prefecture. == Overview == The Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun is considered a family-oriented burial site, with tombs believed to have been constructed in the early 7th century. The cluster comprises 52 tombs, with 44 in the Eastern Cluster and 8 in the Western Cluster. Each tomb is approximately 1 meter in height and width and 2 meters in depth, carved into the sloped pumice tuff bedrock facing south. By modern times, the tombs were already exposed, with their original form lost. Artifacts that could have been buried with the deceased are no longer present, making detailed research challenging. However, the tomb designs, including some with side channels on the floors, indicate advanced craftsmanship characteristic of the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Many of the tomb walls feature carvings of {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩}} and {{ill|Hayagriva (Buddhism)|lt=Bato Kannon|ja|馬頭観音}}, believed to date back to the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} through the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. This suggests prolonged exposure to the elements and considerable cultural use, as evidenced by the inclusion of the site in local folklore, such as the {{ill|Hyakumoku Densetsu (One Hundred Eyes Legend)|ja|百目鬼伝説}}. File:Nagaoka hyakuana inside.jpg|Interior view File:Nagaoka hyakuana monument.jpg|Poetic inscription by {{ill|Misai Kosugi|ja|小杉放庵}} == Surrounding Environment == The Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun is situated in the northern part of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, at the southwestern foothills of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}, near {{ill|Tomatsuriyama|ja|戸祭山}}. The site is located on a plateau flanked by the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川}} and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川}}. The surrounding area features several other tumuli and ancient sites, including the {{ill|Taniguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, as well as remains from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. These sites are linked by the {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}}. The tombs are surrounded by greenery, including {{ill|Quercus serrata|ja|コナラ}} and {{ill|Quercus acutissima|ja|クヌギ}}. The area is designated as the Nagaoka Green Conservation Area by Tochigi Prefecture{{Cite web |url=https://www.pref.tochigi.lg.jp/d04/30jikannti/10nagaoka.html|title=10 Nagaoka Green Conservation Area|website=Tochigi Prefecture |accessdate=2024-11-11}}. Urbanization has increased in the surrounding area with the development of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}. == Cultural Heritage == === Tochigi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Sites ** Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun - Designated on July 26, 1955. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yomiuri Shimbun, Tochigi Edition, May 8, 2016, p. 27. == Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR West Exit): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} to the Teikyo University/Utsunomiya Museum of Art line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Toyosatodai Entrance" and walk for 20 minutes. ; By Car * From National Route 119 (Toyosato Bypass): ** Proceed north from the Nagaokamachi Intersection along the {{ill|Nagaoka Road|ja|長岡街道}} for 3 minutes. * From {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Route 63|ja|栃木県道63号藤原宇都宮線}}: ** Head west from the Sekibori-cho North Intersection via Nagaoka Road for 3 minutes. == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Nagaoka Hyakuana}} * {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}} * {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}} * {{ill|Taniguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}} * {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}} * {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} * {{ill|Hyakumoku|ja|百目鬼}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun}} [[Category:Rock-cut tombs]] [[Category:Ancient tombs in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Utsunomiya]] [[Category:Tochigi Prefecture-designated historic sites]] [[Category:Utsunomiya Scenic Spots]] [[Category:Link complete]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]][[ja:%E9%95%B7%E5%B2%A1%E7%99%BE%E7%A9%B4%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:&action=edit]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun | image = [[File:Nagaoka Hyakuana, higashi-gun-2.jpg|280px]]
Eastern Cluster
[[File:Nagaoka Hyakuana, nishi-gun.jpg|280px]]

File:Nagaoka hyakuana inside.jpg|Interior view File:Nagaoka hyakuana monument.jpg|Poetic inscription by {{ill|Misai Kosugi|ja|小杉放庵}} == Surrounding Environment == The Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun is situated in the northern part of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, at the southwestern foothills of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}, near {{ill|Tomatsuriyama|ja|戸祭山}}. These sites are linked by the {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}}. The tombs are surrounded by greenery, including {{ill|Quercus serrata|ja|コナラ}} and {{ill|Quercus acutissima|ja|クヌギ}}. Urbanization has increased in the surrounding area with the development of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}. == Cultural Heritage == === Tochigi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Sites ** Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun - Designated on July 26, 1955. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yomiuri Shimbun, Tochigi Edition, May 8, 2016, p. 27. == Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR West Exit): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} to the Teikyo University/Utsunomiya Museum of Art line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Toyosatodai Entrance" and walk for 20 minutes. ; By Car * From National Route 119 (Toyosato Bypass): ** Proceed north from the Nagaokamachi Intersection along the {{ill|Nagaoka Road|ja|長岡街道}} for 3 minutes. * From {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Route 63|ja|栃木県道63号藤原宇都宮線}}: ** Head west from the Sekibori-cho North Intersection via Nagaoka Road for 3 minutes. == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Nagaoka Hyakuana}} * {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}} * {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}} * {{ill|Taniguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}} * {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}} * {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} * {{ill|Hyakumoku|ja|百目鬼}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun}} [[Category:Rock-cut tombs]] [[Category:Ancient tombs in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Utsunomiya]] [[Category:Tochigi Prefecture-designated historic sites]] [[Category:Utsunomiya Scenic Spots]] [[Category:Link complete]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]][[ja:%E9%95%B7%E5%B2%A1%E7%99%BE%E7%A9%B4%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[ja:&action=edit]]

Alight at "Toyosatodai Entrance" and walk for 20 minutes. ; By Car * From National Route 119 (Toyosato Bypass): ** Proceed north from the Nagaokamachi Intersection along the {{ill|Nagaoka Road|ja|長岡街道}} for 3 minutes. * From {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Route 63|ja|栃木県道63号藤原宇都宮線}}: ** Head west from the Sekibori-cho North Intersection via Nagaoka Road for 3 minutes. == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Nagaoka Hyakuana}} * {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}} * {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}} * {{ill|Taniguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}} * {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}} * {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} * {{ill|Hyakumoku|ja|百目鬼}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun}} [[Category:Rock-cut tombs]] [[Category:Ancient tombs in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Utsunomiya]] [[Category:Tochigi Prefecture-designated historic sites]] [[Category:Utsunomiya Scenic Spots]] [[Category:Link complete]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]][[ja:%E9%95%B7%E5%B2%A1%E7%99%BE%E7%A9%B4%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[ja:&action=edit]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun | image = [[File:Nagaoka Hyakuana, higashi-gun-2.jpg|280px]]
Eastern Cluster
[[File:Nagaoka Hyakuana, nishi-gun.jpg|280px]]
Western Cluster | location = {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Nagaokachō | Coordinates = {{Coord|36|35|35|N|139|53|3|E}} | Shape = Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster | Dimensions = 52 tombs (44 in the Eastern Cluster, 8 in the Western Cluster) | Construction Era = Early 7th century | Buried Figure = | Cultural Heritage Designation = Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun) | Artifacts = }} '''Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun''' (長岡百穴古墳, also known as Nagaoka Hyakuana or the Nagaoka Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster) is a cluster of {{ill|rock-cut tombs|ja|横穴墓}} located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is designated as a historic site by the Tochigi Prefecture. == Overview == The Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun is considered a family-oriented burial site, with tombs believed to have been constructed in the early 7th century. The cluster comprises 52 tombs, with 44 in the Eastern Cluster and 8 in the Western Cluster. Each tomb is approximately 1 meter in height and width and 2 meters in depth, carved into the sloped pumice tuff bedrock facing south. By modern times, the tombs were already exposed, with their original form lost. Artifacts that could have been buried with the deceased are no longer present, making detailed research challenging. However, the tomb designs, including some with side channels on the floors, indicate advanced craftsmanship characteristic of the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Many of the tomb walls feature carvings of {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩}} and {{ill|Hayagriva (Buddhism)|lt=Bato Kannon|ja|馬頭観音}}, believed to date back to the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} through the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. This suggests prolonged exposure to the elements and considerable cultural use, as evidenced by the inclusion of the site in local folklore, such as the {{ill|Hyakumoku Densetsu (One Hundred Eyes Legend)|ja|百目鬼伝説}}. File:Nagaoka hyakuana inside.jpg|Interior view File:Nagaoka hyakuana monument.jpg|Poetic inscription by {{ill|Misai Kosugi|ja|小杉放庵}} == Surrounding Environment == The Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun is situated in the northern part of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, at the southwestern foothills of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}, near {{ill|Tomatsuriyama|ja|戸祭山}}. The site is located on a plateau flanked by the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川}} and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川}}. The surrounding area features several other tumuli and ancient sites, including the {{ill|Taniguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, as well as remains from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. These sites are linked by the {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}}. The tombs are surrounded by greenery, including {{ill|Quercus serrata|ja|コナラ}} and {{ill|Quercus acutissima|ja|クヌギ}}. The area is designated as the Nagaoka Green Conservation Area by Tochigi Prefecture{{Cite web |url=https://www.pref.tochigi.lg.jp/d04/30jikannti/10nagaoka.html|title=10 Nagaoka Green Conservation Area|website=Tochigi Prefecture |accessdate=2024-11-11}}. Urbanization has increased in the surrounding area with the development of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}. == Cultural Heritage == === Tochigi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Sites ** Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun - Designated on July 26, 1955. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yomiuri Shimbun, Tochigi Edition, May 8, 2016, p. 27. == Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR West Exit): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} to the Teikyo University/Utsunomiya Museum of Art line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Toyosatodai Entrance" and walk for 20 minutes. ; By Car * From National Route 119 (Toyosato Bypass): ** Proceed north from the Nagaokamachi Intersection along the {{ill|Nagaoka Road|ja|長岡街道}} for 3 minutes. * From {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Route 63|ja|栃木県道63号藤原宇都宮線}}: ** Head west from the Sekibori-cho North Intersection via Nagaoka Road for 3 minutes. == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Nagaoka Hyakuana}} * {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}} * {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}} * {{ill|Taniguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}} * {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}} * {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} * {{ill|Hyakumoku|ja|百目鬼}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:Rock-cut tombs]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Ancient tombs in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:History of Utsunomiya]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Tochigi Prefecture-designated historic sites]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Utsunomiya Scenic Spots]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]][[ja:%E9%95%B7%E5%B2%A1%E7%99%BE%E7%A9%B4%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]][[Category:dd]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[ja:&action=edit]]

Urbanization has increased in the surrounding area with the development of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}. == Cultural Heritage == === Tochigi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Sites ** Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun - Designated on July 26, 1955. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yomiuri Shimbun, Tochigi Edition, May 8, 2016, p. 27. == Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR West Exit): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} to the Teikyo University/Utsunomiya Museum of Art line (approx. 15 minutes). {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun | image = [[File:Nagaoka Hyakuana, higashi-gun-2.jpg|280px]]
Eastern Cluster
[[File:Nagaoka Hyakuana, nishi-gun.jpg|280px]]
Western Cluster | location = {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Nagaokachō | Coordinates = {{Coord|36|35|35|N|139|53|3|E}} | Shape = Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster | Dimensions = 52 tombs (44 in the Eastern Cluster, 8 in the Western Cluster) | Construction Era = Early 7th century | Buried Figure = | Cultural Heritage Designation = Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun) | Artifacts = }} '''Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun''' (長岡百穴古墳, also known as Nagaoka Hyakuana or the Nagaoka Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster) is a cluster of {{ill|rock-cut tombs|ja|横穴墓}} located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is designated as a historic site by the Tochigi Prefecture. == Overview == The Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun is considered a family-oriented burial site, with tombs believed to have been constructed in the early 7th century. The cluster comprises 52 tombs, with 44 in the Eastern Cluster and 8 in the Western Cluster. Each tomb is approximately 1 meter in height and width and 2 meters in depth, carved into the sloped pumice tuff bedrock facing south. By modern times, the tombs were already exposed, with their original form lost. Artifacts that could have been buried with the deceased are no longer present, making detailed research challenging. However, the tomb designs, including some with side channels on the floors, indicate advanced craftsmanship characteristic of the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Many of the tomb walls feature carvings of {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩}} and {{ill|Hayagriva (Buddhism)|lt=Bato Kannon|ja|馬頭観音}}, believed to date back to the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} through the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. This suggests prolonged exposure to the elements and considerable cultural use, as evidenced by the inclusion of the site in local folklore, such as the {{ill|Hyakumoku Densetsu (One Hundred Eyes Legend)|ja|百目鬼伝説}}. File:Nagaoka hyakuana inside.jpg|Interior view File:Nagaoka hyakuana monument.jpg|Poetic inscription by {{ill|Misai Kosugi|ja|小杉放庵}} == Surrounding Environment == The Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun is situated in the northern part of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, at the southwestern foothills of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}, near {{ill|Tomatsuriyama|ja|戸祭山}}. The site is located on a plateau flanked by the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川}} and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川}}. The surrounding area features several other tumuli and ancient sites, including the {{ill|Taniguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, as well as remains from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. These sites are linked by the {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}}. The tombs are surrounded by greenery, including {{ill|Quercus serrata|ja|コナラ}} and {{ill|Quercus acutissima|ja|クヌギ}}. The area is designated as the Nagaoka Green Conservation Area by Tochigi Prefecture{{Cite web |url=https://www.pref.tochigi.lg.jp/d04/30jikannti/10nagaoka.html|title=10 Nagaoka Green Conservation Area|website=Tochigi Prefecture |accessdate=2024-11-11}}. Urbanization has increased in the surrounding area with the development of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}. == Cultural Heritage == === Tochigi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Sites ** Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun - Designated on July 26, 1955. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yomiuri Shimbun, Tochigi Edition, May 8, 2016, p. 27. == Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR West Exit): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} to the Teikyo University/Utsunomiya Museum of Art line (approx. 15 minutes). The site is located on a plateau flanked by the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川}} and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川}}. The surrounding area features several other tumuli and ancient sites, including the {{ill|Taniguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, as well as remains from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. These sites are linked by the {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}}. The tombs are surrounded by greenery, including {{ill|Quercus serrata|ja|コナラ}} and {{ill|Quercus acutissima|ja|クヌギ}}. The area is designated as the Nagaoka Green Conservation Area by Tochigi Prefecture{{Cite web |url=https://www.pref.tochigi.lg.jp/d04/30jikannti/10nagaoka.html|title=10 Nagaoka Green Conservation Area|website=Tochigi Prefecture |accessdate=2024-11-11}}. Western Cluster | location = {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Nagaokachō | Coordinates = {{Coord|36|35|35|N|139|53|3|E}} | Shape = Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster | Dimensions = 52 tombs (44 in the Eastern Cluster, 8 in the Western Cluster) | Construction Era = Early 7th century | Buried Figure = | Cultural Heritage Designation = Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun) | Artifacts = }} '''Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun''' (長岡百穴古墳, also known as Nagaoka Hyakuana or the Nagaoka Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster) is a cluster of {{ill|rock-cut tombs|ja|横穴墓}} located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is designated as a historic site by the Tochigi Prefecture. == Overview == The Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun is considered a family-oriented burial site, with tombs believed to have been constructed in the early 7th century. The cluster comprises 52 tombs, with 44 in the Eastern Cluster and 8 in the Western Cluster. Each tomb is approximately 1 meter in height and width and 2 meters in depth, carved into the sloped pumice tuff bedrock facing south. By modern times, the tombs were already exposed, with their original form lost. Artifacts that could have been buried with the deceased are no longer present, making detailed research challenging. However, the tomb designs, including some with side channels on the floors, indicate advanced craftsmanship characteristic of the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Many of the tomb walls feature carvings of {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩}} and {{ill|Hayagriva (Buddhism)|lt=Bato Kannon|ja|馬頭観音}}, believed to date back to the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} through the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. This suggests prolonged exposure to the elements and considerable cultural use, as evidenced by the inclusion of the site in local folklore, such as the {{ill|Hyakumoku Densetsu (One Hundred Eyes Legend)|ja|百目鬼伝説}}. These sites are linked by the {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}}. The tombs are surrounded by greenery, including {{ill|Quercus serrata|ja|コナラ}} and {{ill|Quercus acutissima|ja|クヌギ}}. The area is designated as the Nagaoka Green Conservation Area by Tochigi Prefecture{{Cite web |url=https://www.pref.tochigi.lg.jp/d04/30jikannti/10nagaoka.html|title=10 Nagaoka Green Conservation Area|website=Tochigi Prefecture |accessdate=2024-11-11}}. It is designated as a historic site by the Tochigi Prefecture. == Overview == The Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun is considered a family-oriented burial site, with tombs believed to have been constructed in the early 7th century. The cluster comprises 52 tombs, with 44 in the Eastern Cluster and 8 in the Western Cluster. Each tomb is approximately 1 meter in height and width and 2 meters in depth, carved into the sloped pumice tuff bedrock facing south. By modern times, the tombs were already exposed, with their original form lost. Artifacts that could have been buried with the deceased are no longer present, making detailed research challenging. However, the tomb designs, including some with side channels on the floors, indicate advanced craftsmanship characteristic of the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Many of the tomb walls feature carvings of {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩}} and {{ill|Hayagriva (Buddhism)|lt=Bato Kannon|ja|馬頭観音}}, believed to date back to the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} through the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. This suggests prolonged exposure to the elements and considerable cultural use, as evidenced by the inclusion of the site in local folklore, such as the {{ill|Hyakumoku Densetsu (One Hundred Eyes Legend)|ja|百目鬼伝説}}. File:Nagaoka hyakuana inside.jpg|Interior view File:Nagaoka hyakuana monument.jpg|Poetic inscription by {{ill|Misai Kosugi|ja|小杉放庵}} == Surrounding Environment == The Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun is situated in the northern part of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, at the southwestern foothills of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}, near {{ill|Tomatsuriyama|ja|戸祭山}}. The site is located on a plateau flanked by the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川}} and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川}}. The surrounding area features several other tumuli and ancient sites, including the {{ill|Taniguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, as well as remains from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. These sites are linked by the {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}}. The tombs are surrounded by greenery, including {{ill|Quercus serrata|ja|コナラ}} and {{ill|Quercus acutissima|ja|クヌギ}}. The area is designated as the Nagaoka Green Conservation Area by Tochigi Prefecture{{Cite web |url=https://www.pref.tochigi.lg.jp/d04/30jikannti/10nagaoka.html|title=10 Nagaoka Green Conservation Area|website=Tochigi Prefecture |accessdate=2024-11-11}}. Urbanization has increased in the surrounding area with the development of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}. == Cultural Heritage == === Tochigi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Sites ** Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun - Designated on July 26, 1955. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yomiuri Shimbun, Tochigi Edition, May 8, 2016, p. 27. == Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR West Exit): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} to the Teikyo University/Utsunomiya Museum of Art line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Toyosatodai Entrance" and walk for 20 minutes. ; By Car * From National Route 119 (Toyosato Bypass): ** Proceed north from the Nagaokamachi Intersection along the {{ill|Nagaoka Road|ja|長岡街道}} for 3 minutes. * From {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Route 63|ja|栃木県道63号藤原宇都宮線}}: ** Head west from the Sekibori-cho North Intersection via Nagaoka Road for 3 minutes. == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Nagaoka Hyakuana}} * {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}} * {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}} * {{ill|Taniguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}} * {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}} * {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} * {{ill|Hyakumoku|ja|百目鬼}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rock-cut tombs]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient tombs in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Utsunomiya]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Tochigi Prefecture-designated historic sites]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Utsunomiya Scenic Spots]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]][[ja:%E9%95%B7%E5%B2%A1%E7%99%BE%E7%A9%B4%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Nagaoka Hyakka Kofun]][[ja:&action=edit]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun | image = [[File:Nagaoka Hyakuana, higashi-gun-2.jpg|280px]]
Eastern Cluster
[[File:Nagaoka Hyakuana, nishi-gun.jpg|280px]]
Western Cluster | location = {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Nagaokachō | Coordinates = {{Coord|36|35|35|N|139|53|3|E}} | Shape = Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster | Dimensions = 52 tombs (44 in the Eastern Cluster, 8 in the Western Cluster) | Construction Era = Early 7th century | Buried Figure = | Cultural Heritage Designation = Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun) | Artifacts = }} '''Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun''' (長岡百穴古墳, also known as Nagaoka Hyakuana or the Nagaoka Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster) is a cluster of {{ill|rock-cut tombs|ja|横穴墓}} located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is designated as a historic site by the Tochigi Prefecture. == Overview == The Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun is considered a family-oriented burial site, with tombs believed to have been constructed in the early 7th century. The cluster comprises 52 tombs, with 44 in the Eastern Cluster and 8 in the Western Cluster. Each tomb is approximately 1 meter in height and width and 2 meters in depth, carved into the sloped pumice tuff bedrock facing south. By modern times, the tombs were already exposed, with their original form lost. Artifacts that could have been buried with the deceased are no longer present, making detailed research challenging. However, the tomb designs, including some with side channels on the floors, indicate advanced craftsmanship characteristic of the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Many of the tomb walls feature carvings of {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩}} and {{ill|Hayagriva (Buddhism)|lt=Bato Kannon|ja|馬頭観音}}, believed to date back to the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} through the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. This suggests prolonged exposure to the elements and considerable cultural use, as evidenced by the inclusion of the site in local folklore, such as the {{ill|Hyakumoku Densetsu (One Hundred Eyes Legend)|ja|百目鬼伝説}}. File:Nagaoka hyakuana inside.jpg|Interior view File:Nagaoka hyakuana monument.jpg|Poetic inscription by {{ill|Misai Kosugi|ja|小杉放庵}} == Surrounding Environment == The Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun is situated in the northern part of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, at the southwestern foothills of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}, near {{ill|Tomatsuriyama|ja|戸祭山}}. The site is located on a plateau flanked by the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川}} and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川}}. The surrounding area features several other tumuli and ancient sites, including the {{ill|Taniguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, as well as remains from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. These sites are linked by the {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}}. The tombs are surrounded by greenery, including {{ill|Quercus serrata|ja|コナラ}} and {{ill|Quercus acutissima|ja|クヌギ}}. The area is designated as the Nagaoka Green Conservation Area by Tochigi Prefecture{{Cite web |url=https://www.pref.tochigi.lg.jp/d04/30jikannti/10nagaoka.html|title=10 Nagaoka Green Conservation Area|website=Tochigi Prefecture |accessdate=2024-11-11}}. Urbanization has increased in the surrounding area with the development of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}. == Cultural Heritage == === Tochigi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Sites ** Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun - Designated on July 26, 1955. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yomiuri Shimbun, Tochigi Edition, May 8, 2016, p. 27. == Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR West Exit): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} to the Teikyo University/Utsunomiya Museum of Art line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Toyosatodai Entrance" and walk for 20 minutes. ; By Car * From National Route 119 (Toyosato Bypass): ** Proceed north from the Nagaokamachi Intersection along the {{ill|Nagaoka Road|ja|長岡街道}} for 3 minutes. * From {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Route 63|ja|栃木県道63号藤原宇都宮線}}: ** Head west from the Sekibori-cho North Intersection via Nagaoka Road for 3 minutes. == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Nagaoka Hyakuana}} * {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}} * {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}} * {{ill|Taniguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}} * {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}} * {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} * {{ill|Hyakumoku|ja|百目鬼}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rock-cut tombs]] Eastern Cluster
[[File:Nagaoka Hyakuana, nishi-gun.jpg|280px]]
Western Cluster | location = {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Nagaokachō | Coordinates = {{Coord|36|35|35|N|139|53|3|E}} | Shape = Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster | Dimensions = 52 tombs (44 in the Eastern Cluster, 8 in the Western Cluster) | Construction Era = Early 7th century | Buried Figure = | Cultural Heritage Designation = Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun) | Artifacts = }} '''Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun''' (長岡百穴古墳, also known as Nagaoka Hyakuana or the Nagaoka Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster) is a cluster of {{ill|rock-cut tombs|ja|横穴墓}} located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is designated as a historic site by the Tochigi Prefecture. == Overview == The Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun is considered a family-oriented burial site, with tombs believed to have been constructed in the early 7th century. The cluster comprises 52 tombs, with 44 in the Eastern Cluster and 8 in the Western Cluster. Each tomb is approximately 1 meter in height and width and 2 meters in depth, carved into the sloped pumice tuff bedrock facing south. By modern times, the tombs were already exposed, with their original form lost. Artifacts that could have been buried with the deceased are no longer present, making detailed research challenging. However, the tomb designs, including some with side channels on the floors, indicate advanced craftsmanship characteristic of the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Many of the tomb walls feature carvings of {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩}} and {{ill|Hayagriva (Buddhism)|lt=Bato Kannon|ja|馬頭観音}}, believed to date back to the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} through the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. This suggests prolonged exposure to the elements and considerable cultural use, as evidenced by the inclusion of the site in local folklore, such as the {{ill|Hyakumoku Densetsu (One Hundred Eyes Legend)|ja|百目鬼伝説}}. File:Nagaoka hyakuana inside.jpg|Interior view File:Nagaoka hyakuana monument.jpg|Poetic inscription by {{ill|Misai Kosugi|ja|小杉放庵}} == Surrounding Environment == The Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun is situated in the northern part of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, at the southwestern foothills of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}, near {{ill|Tomatsuriyama|ja|戸祭山}}. The site is located on a plateau flanked by the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川}} and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川}}. The surrounding area features several other tumuli and ancient sites, including the {{ill|Taniguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, as well as remains from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. These sites are linked by the {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}}. The tombs are surrounded by greenery, including {{ill|Quercus serrata|ja|コナラ}} and {{ill|Quercus acutissima|ja|クヌギ}}. The area is designated as the Nagaoka Green Conservation Area by Tochigi Prefecture{{Cite web |url=https://www.pref.tochigi.lg.jp/d04/30jikannti/10nagaoka.html|title=10 Nagaoka Green Conservation Area|website=Tochigi Prefecture |accessdate=2024-11-11}}. Urbanization has increased in the surrounding area with the development of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}. == Cultural Heritage == === Tochigi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Sites ** Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun - Designated on July 26, 1955. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yomiuri Shimbun, Tochigi Edition, May 8, 2016, p. 27. == Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR West Exit): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} to the Teikyo University/Utsunomiya Museum of Art line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Toyosatodai Entrance" and walk for 20 minutes. ; By Car * From National Route 119 (Toyosato Bypass): ** Proceed north from the Nagaokamachi Intersection along the {{ill|Nagaoka Road|ja|長岡街道}} for 3 minutes. * From {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Route 63|ja|栃木県道63号藤原宇都宮線}}: ** Head west from the Sekibori-cho North Intersection via Nagaoka Road for 3 minutes. == Related Articles == These sites are linked by the {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}}. The tombs are surrounded by greenery, including {{ill|Quercus serrata|ja|コナラ}} and {{ill|Quercus acutissima|ja|クヌギ}}. The area is designated as the Nagaoka Green Conservation Area by Tochigi Prefecture{{Cite web |url=https://www.pref.tochigi.lg.jp/d04/30jikannti/10nagaoka.html|title=10 Nagaoka Green Conservation Area|website=Tochigi Prefecture |accessdate=2024-11-11}}. Urbanization has increased in the surrounding area with the development of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}. == Cultural Heritage == === Tochigi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Sites ** Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun - Designated on July 26, 1955. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yomiuri Shimbun, Tochigi Edition, May 8, 2016, p. 27. == Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR West Exit): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} to the Teikyo University/Utsunomiya Museum of Art line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Toyosatodai Entrance" and walk for 20 minutes. ; By Car * From National Route 119 (Toyosato Bypass): ** Proceed north from the Nagaokamachi Intersection along the {{ill|Nagaoka Road|ja|長岡街道}} for 3 minutes. * From {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Route 63|ja|栃木県道63号藤原宇都宮線}}: ** Head west from the Sekibori-cho North Intersection via Nagaoka Road for 3 minutes. == Related Articles == {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun | image = [[File:Nagaoka Hyakuana, higashi-gun-2.jpg|280px]]
Eastern Cluster
[[File:Nagaoka Hyakuana, nishi-gun.jpg|280px]]
Western Cluster | location = {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Nagaokachō | Coordinates = {{Coord|36|35|35|N|139|53|3|E}} | Shape = Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster | Dimensions = 52 tombs (44 in the Eastern Cluster, 8 in the Western Cluster) | Construction Era = Early 7th century | Buried Figure = | Cultural Heritage Designation = Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun) | Artifacts = }} '''Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun''' (長岡百穴古墳, also known as Nagaoka Hyakuana or the Nagaoka Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster) is a cluster of {{ill|rock-cut tombs|ja|横穴墓}} located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is designated as a historic site by the Tochigi Prefecture. == Overview == The Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun is considered a family-oriented burial site, with tombs believed to have been constructed in the early 7th century. The cluster comprises 52 tombs, with 44 in the Eastern Cluster and 8 in the Western Cluster. Each tomb is approximately 1 meter in height and width and 2 meters in depth, carved into the sloped pumice tuff bedrock facing south. By modern times, the tombs were already exposed, with their original form lost. Artifacts that could have been buried with the deceased are no longer present, making detailed research challenging. However, the tomb designs, including some with side channels on the floors, indicate advanced craftsmanship characteristic of the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Many of the tomb walls feature carvings of {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩}} and {{ill|Hayagriva (Buddhism)|lt=Bato Kannon|ja|馬頭観音}}, believed to date back to the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} through the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. This suggests prolonged exposure to the elements and considerable cultural use, as evidenced by the inclusion of the site in local folklore, such as the {{ill|Hyakumoku Densetsu (One Hundred Eyes Legend)|ja|百目鬼伝説}}. File:Nagaoka hyakuana inside.jpg|Interior view File:Nagaoka hyakuana monument.jpg|Poetic inscription by {{ill|Misai Kosugi|ja|小杉放庵}} == Surrounding Environment == The Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun is situated in the northern part of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, at the southwestern foothills of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}, near {{ill|Tomatsuriyama|ja|戸祭山}}. The site is located on a plateau flanked by the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川}} and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川}}. The surrounding area features several other tumuli and ancient sites, including the {{ill|Taniguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, as well as remains from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. These sites are linked by the {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}}. The tombs are surrounded by greenery, including {{ill|Quercus serrata|ja|コナラ}} and {{ill|Quercus acutissima|ja|クヌギ}}. The area is designated as the Nagaoka Green Conservation Area by Tochigi Prefecture{{Cite web |url=https://www.pref.tochigi.lg.jp/d04/30jikannti/10nagaoka.html|title=10 Nagaoka Green Conservation Area|website=Tochigi Prefecture |accessdate=2024-11-11}}. Urbanization has increased in the surrounding area with the development of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}. == Cultural Heritage == === Tochigi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Sites ** Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun - Designated on July 26, 1955. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yomiuri Shimbun, Tochigi Edition, May 8, 2016, p. 27. == Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR West Exit): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} to the Teikyo University/Utsunomiya Museum of Art line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Toyosatodai Entrance" and walk for 20 minutes. ; By Car * From National Route 119 (Toyosato Bypass): ** Proceed north from the Nagaokamachi Intersection along the {{ill|Nagaoka Road|ja|長岡街道}} for 3 minutes. * From {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Route 63|ja|栃木県道63号藤原宇都宮線}}: ** Head west from the Sekibori-cho North Intersection via Nagaoka Road for 3 minutes. == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Nagaoka Hyakuana}} * {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}} * {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}} * {{ill|Taniguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}} * {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}} * {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} * {{ill|Hyakumoku|ja|百目鬼}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rock-cut tombs]]
