revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun | image = [[File:Nagaoka Hyakuana, higashi-gun-2.jpg|280px]]
Eastern Cluster
[[File:Nagaoka Hyakuana, nishi-gun.jpg|280px]]
Western Cluster | location = {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Nagaokachō | Coordinates = {{Coord|36|35|35|N|139|53|3|E}} | Shape = Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster | Dimensions = 52 tombs (44 in the Eastern Cluster, 8 in the Western Cluster) | Construction Era = Early 7th century | Buried Figure = | Cultural Heritage Designation = Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun) | Artifacts = }} '''Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun''' (長岡百穴古墳, also known as Nagaoka Hyakuana or the Nagaoka Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster) is a cluster of {{ill|rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}} located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is designated as a historic site by the Tochigi Prefecture. == Overview == The Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun is considered a family-oriented burial site, with tombs believed to have been constructed in the early 7th century. The cluster comprises 52 tombs, with 44 in the Eastern Cluster and 8 in the Western Cluster. Each tomb is approximately 1 meter in height and width and 2 meters in depth, carved into the sloped pumice tuff bedrock facing south. By modern times, the tombs were already exposed, with their original form lost. Artifacts that could have been buried with the deceased are no longer present, making detailed research challenging. However, the tomb designs, including some with side channels on the floors, indicate advanced craftsmanship characteristic of the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Many of the tomb walls feature carvings of {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩}} and {{ill|Hayagriva (Buddhism)|lt=Bato Kannon|ja|馬頭観音}}, believed to date back to the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} through the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. This suggests prolonged exposure to the elements and considerable cultural use, as evidenced by the inclusion of the site in local folklore, such as the {{ill|Hyakumoku Densetsu (One Hundred Eyes Legend)|ja|百目鬼伝説}}. File:Nagaoka hyakuana inside.jpg|Interior view File:Nagaoka hyakuana monument.jpg|Poetic inscription by {{ill|Misai Kosugi|ja|小杉放庵}} == Surrounding Environment == The Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun is situated in the northern part of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, at the southwestern foothills of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}, near {{ill|Tomatsuriyama|ja|戸祭山}}. The site is located on a plateau flanked by the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川}} and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川}}. The surrounding area features several other tumuli and ancient sites, including the {{ill|Taniguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, as well as remains from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. These sites are linked by the {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}}. The tombs are surrounded by greenery, including {{ill|Quercus serrata|ja|コナラ}} and {{ill|Quercus acutissima|ja|クヌギ}}. The area is designated as the Nagaoka Green Conservation Area by Tochigi Prefecture{{Cite web |url=https://www.pref.tochigi.lg.jp/d04/30jikannti/10nagaoka.html|title=10 Nagaoka Green Conservation Area|website=Tochigi Prefecture |accessdate=2024-11-11}}. Urbanization has increased in the surrounding area with the development of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}. == Cultural Heritage == === Tochigi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Sites ** Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun - Designated on July 26, 1955. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yomiuri Shimbun, Tochigi Edition, May 8, 2016, p. 27. == Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR West Exit): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} to the Teikyo University/Utsunomiya Museum of Art line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Toyosatodai Entrance" and walk for 20 minutes. ; By Car * From National Route 119 (Toyosato Bypass): ** Proceed north from the Nagaokamachi Intersection along the {{ill|Nagaoka Road|ja|長岡街道}} for 3 minutes. * From {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Route 63|ja|栃木県道63号藤原宇都宮線}}: ** Head west from the Sekibori-cho North Intersection via Nagaoka Road for 3 minutes. == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Nagaoka Hyakuana}} * {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}} * {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}} * {{ill|Taniguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}} * {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}} * {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} * {{ill|Hyakumoku|ja|百目鬼}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun}} [[Category:Rock-cut tombs]] [[Category:Ancient tombs in Tochigi Prefecture]] Many of the tomb walls feature carvings of {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩}} and {{ill|Hayagriva (Buddhism)|lt=Bato Kannon|ja|馬頭観音}}, believed to date back to the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} through the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. This suggests prolonged exposure to the elements and considerable cultural use, as evidenced by the inclusion of the site in local folklore, such as the {{ill|Hyakumoku Densetsu (One Hundred Eyes Legend)|ja|百目鬼伝説}}. File:Nagaoka hyakuana inside.jpg|Interior view File:Nagaoka hyakuana monument.jpg|Poetic inscription by {{ill|Misai Kosugi|ja|小杉放庵}} == Surrounding Environment == The Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun is situated in the northern part of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, at the southwestern foothills of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}, near {{ill|Tomatsuriyama|ja|戸祭山}}. The site is located on a plateau flanked by the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川}} and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川}}. The surrounding area features several other tumuli and ancient sites, including the {{ill|Taniguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, as well as remains from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. These sites are linked by the {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}}. The tombs are surrounded by greenery, including {{ill|Quercus serrata|ja|コナラ}} and {{ill|Quercus acutissima|ja|クヌギ}}. The area is designated as the Nagaoka Green Conservation Area by Tochigi Prefecture{{Cite web |url=https://www.pref.tochigi.lg.jp/d04/30jikannti/10nagaoka.html|title=10 Nagaoka Green Conservation Area|website=Tochigi Prefecture |accessdate=2024-11-11}}. Urbanization has increased in the surrounding area with the development of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}. == Cultural Heritage == === Tochigi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Sites ** Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun - Designated on July 26, 1955. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yomiuri Shimbun, Tochigi Edition, May 8, 2016, p. 27. == Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR West Exit): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} to the Teikyo University/Utsunomiya Museum of Art line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Toyosatodai Entrance" and walk for 20 minutes. ; By Car * From National Route 119 (Toyosato Bypass): ** Proceed north from the Nagaokamachi Intersection along the {{ill|Nagaoka Road|ja|長岡街道}} for 3 minutes. * From {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Route 63|ja|栃木県道63号藤原宇都宮線}}: ** Head west from the Sekibori-cho North Intersection via Nagaoka Road for 3 minutes. == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Nagaoka Hyakuana}} * {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}} * {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}} * {{ill|Taniguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}} * {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}} * {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} * {{ill|Hyakumoku|ja|百目鬼}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun}} [[Category:Rock-cut tombs]] [[Category:Ancient tombs in Tochigi Prefecture]] But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun | image = [[File:Nagaoka Hyakuana, higashi-gun-2.jpg|280px]]
Eastern Cluster
[[File:Nagaoka Hyakuana, nishi-gun.jpg|280px]]
Western Cluster | location = {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Nagaokachō | coordinates = {{Coord|36|35|35|N|139|53|3|E}} | Shape = Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster | area = 52 tombs (44 in the Eastern Cluster, 8 in the Western Cluster) | Construction Era = Early 7th century | Buried Figure = | Cultural Heritage Designation = Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun) | Artifacts = }} '''Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun''' (長岡百穴古墳, also known as Nagaoka Hyakuana or the Nagaoka Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster) is a cluster of {{ill|rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}} located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is designated as a historic site by the Tochigi Prefecture. == Overview == The Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun is considered a family-oriented burial site, with tombs believed to have been constructed in the early 7th century. The cluster comprises 52 tombs, with 44 in the Eastern Cluster and 8 in the Western Cluster. Each tomb is approximately 1 meter in height and width and 2 meters in depth, carved into the sloped pumice tuff bedrock facing south. By modern times, the tombs were already exposed, with their original form lost. Artifacts that could have been buried with the deceased are no longer present, making detailed research challenging. However, the tomb designs, including some with side channels on the floors, indicate advanced craftsmanship characteristic of the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Many of the tomb walls feature carvings of {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩}} and {{ill|Hayagriva (Buddhism)|lt=Bato Kannon|ja|馬頭観音}}, believed to date back to the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} through the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. This suggests prolonged exposure to the elements and considerable cultural use, as evidenced by the inclusion of the site in local folklore, such as the {{ill|Hyakumoku Densetsu (One Hundred Eyes Legend)|ja|百目鬼伝説}}. File:Nagaoka hyakuana inside.jpg|Interior view File:Nagaoka hyakuana monument.jpg|Poetic inscription by {{ill|Misai Kosugi|ja|小杉放庵}} == Surrounding Environment == The Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun is situated in the northern part of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, at the southwestern foothills of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}, near {{ill|Tomatsuriyama|ja|戸祭山}}. The site is located on a plateau flanked by the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川}} and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川}}. The surrounding area features several other tumuli and ancient sites, including the {{ill|Taniguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, as well as remains from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. These sites are linked by the {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}}. The tombs are surrounded by greenery, including {{ill|Quercus serrata|ja|コナラ}} and {{ill|Quercus acutissima|ja|クヌギ}}. The area is designated as the Nagaoka Green Conservation Area by Tochigi Prefecture{{Cite web |url=https://www.pref.tochigi.lg.jp/d04/30jikannti/10nagaoka.html|title=10 Nagaoka Green Conservation Area|website=Tochigi Prefecture |accessdate=2024-11-11}}. Urbanization has increased in the surrounding area with the development of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}. == Cultural Heritage == === Tochigi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Sites ** Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun - Designated on July 26, 1955. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yomiuri Shimbun, Tochigi Edition, May 8, 2016, p. 27. == Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR West Exit): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} to the Teikyo University/Utsunomiya Museum of Art line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Toyosatodai Entrance" and walk for 20 minutes. ; By Car * From National Route 119 (Toyosato Bypass): ** Proceed north from the Nagaokamachi Intersection along the {{ill|Nagaoka Road|ja|長岡街道}} for 3 minutes. * From {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Route 63|ja|栃木県道63号藤原宇都宮線}}: ** Head west from the Sekibori-cho North Intersection via Nagaoka Road for 3 minutes. == Related Articles == Eastern Cluster
[[File:Nagaoka Hyakuana, nishi-gun.jpg|280px]]
Western Cluster | location = {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Nagaokachō | coordinates = {{Coord|36|35|35|N|139|53|3|E}} | Shape = Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster | area = 52 tombs (44 in the Eastern Cluster, 8 in the Western Cluster) | built = Early 7th century | Buried Figure = | other_designation Designation = Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun) | Artifacts = }} '''Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun''' (長岡百穴古墳, also known as Nagaoka Hyakuana or the Nagaoka Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster) is a cluster of {{ill|rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}} located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is designated as a historic site by the Tochigi Prefecture. == Overview == The Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun is considered a family-oriented burial site, with tombs believed to have been constructed in the early 7th century. The cluster comprises 52 tombs, with 44 in the Eastern Cluster and 8 in the Western Cluster. Each tomb is approximately 1 meter in height and width and 2 meters in depth, carved into the sloped pumice tuff bedrock facing south. By modern times, the tombs were already exposed, with their original form lost. Artifacts that could have been buried with the deceased are no longer present, making detailed research challenging. However, the tomb designs, including some with side channels on the floors, indicate advanced craftsmanship characteristic of the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Many of the tomb walls feature carvings of {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩}} and {{ill|Hayagriva (Buddhism)|lt=Bato Kannon|ja|馬頭観音}}, believed to date back to the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} through the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. This suggests prolonged exposure to the elements and considerable cultural use, as evidenced by the inclusion of the site in local folklore, such as the {{ill|Hyakumoku Densetsu (One Hundred Eyes Legend)|ja|百目鬼伝説}}. File:Nagaoka hyakuana inside.jpg|Interior view File:Nagaoka hyakuana monument.jpg|Poetic inscription by {{ill|Misai Kosugi|ja|小杉放庵}} == Surrounding Environment == The Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun is situated in the northern part of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, at the southwestern foothills of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}, near {{ill|Tomatsuriyama|ja|戸祭山}}. The site is located on a plateau flanked by the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川}} and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川}}. The surrounding area features several other tumuli and ancient sites, including the {{ill|Taniguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, as well as remains from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. These sites are linked by the {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}}. The tombs are surrounded by greenery, including {{ill|Quercus serrata|ja|コナラ}} and {{ill|Quercus acutissima|ja|クヌギ}}. The area is designated as the Nagaoka Green Conservation Area by Tochigi Prefecture{{Cite web |url=https://www.pref.tochigi.lg.jp/d04/30jikannti/10nagaoka.html|title=10 Nagaoka Green Conservation Area|website=Tochigi Prefecture |accessdate=2024-11-11}}. Urbanization has increased in the surrounding area with the development of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}. == other_designation == === Tochigi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Sites ** Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun - Designated on July 26, 1955. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yomiuri Shimbun, Tochigi Edition, May 8, 2016, p. 27. == Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR West Exit): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} to the Teikyo University/Utsunomiya Museum of Art line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Toyosatodai Entrance" and walk for 20 minutes. ; By Car * From National Route 119 (Toyosato Bypass): ** Proceed north from the Nagaokamachi Intersection along the {{ill|Nagaoka Road|ja|長岡街道}} for 3 minutes. * From {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Route 63|ja|栃木県道63号藤原宇都宮線}}: ** Head west from the Sekibori-cho North Intersection via Nagaoka Road for 3 minutes. == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Nagaoka Hyakuana}} * {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}} * {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}} * {{ill|Taniguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}} * {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}} * {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} * {{ill|Hyakumoku|ja|百目鬼}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun}} [[Category:Rock-cut tombs]] [[Category:Ancient tombs in Tochigi Prefecture]] Urbanization has increased in the surrounding area with the development of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}. == other_designation == === Tochigi Prefecture Designated other_designation === * Historic Sites ** Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun - Designated on July 26, 1955. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yomiuri Shimbun, Tochigi Edition, May 8, 2016, p. 27. == Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR West Exit): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} to the Teikyo University/Utsunomiya Museum of Art line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Toyosatodai Entrance" and walk for 20 minutes. ; By Car * From National Route 119 (Toyosato Bypass): ** Proceed north from the Nagaokamachi Intersection along the {{ill|Nagaoka Road|ja|長岡街道}} for 3 minutes. * From {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Route 63|ja|栃木県道63号藤原宇都宮線}}: ** Head west from the Sekibori-cho North Intersection via Nagaoka Road for 3 minutes. == Related Articles == {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun | image = [[File:Nagaoka Hyakuana, higashi-gun-2.jpg|280px]]
Eastern Cluster
[[File:Nagaoka Hyakuana, nishi-gun.jpg|280px]]
Western Cluster | location = {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Nagaokachō | coordinates = {{Coord|36|35|35|N|139|53|3|E}} | type = Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster | area = 52 tombs (44 in the Eastern Cluster, 8 in the Western Cluster) | built = Early 7th century | Buried Figure = | other_designation Designation = Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun) | Artifacts = }} '''Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun''' (長岡百穴古墳, also known as Nagaoka Hyakuana or the Nagaoka Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster) is a cluster of {{ill|rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}} located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is designated as a historic site by the Tochigi Prefecture. == Overview == The Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun is considered a family-oriented burial site, with tombs believed to have been constructed in the early 7th century. The cluster comprises 52 tombs, with 44 in the Eastern Cluster and 8 in the Western Cluster. Each tomb is approximately 1 meter in height and width and 2 meters in depth, carved into the sloped pumice tuff bedrock facing south. By modern times, the tombs were already exposed, with their original form lost. Artifacts that could have been buried with the deceased are no longer present, making detailed research challenging. However, the tomb designs, including some with side channels on the floors, indicate advanced craftsmanship characteristic of the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Many of the tomb walls feature carvings of {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩}} and {{ill|Hayagriva (Buddhism)|lt=Bato Kannon|ja|馬頭観音}}, believed to date back to the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} through the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. This suggests prolonged exposure to the elements and considerable cultural use, as evidenced by the inclusion of the site in local folklore, such as the {{ill|Hyakumoku Densetsu (One Hundred Eyes Legend)|ja|百目鬼伝説}}. File:Nagaoka hyakuana inside.jpg|Interior view File:Nagaoka hyakuana monument.jpg|Poetic inscription by {{ill|Misai Kosugi|ja|小杉放庵}} == Surrounding Environment == The Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun is situated in the northern part of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, at the southwestern foothills of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}, near {{ill|Tomatsuriyama|ja|戸祭山}}. The site is located on a plateau flanked by the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川}} and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川}}. The surrounding area features several other tumuli and ancient sites, including the {{ill|Taniguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, as well as remains from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. These sites are linked by the {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}}. The tombs are surrounded by greenery, including {{ill|Quercus serrata|ja|コナラ}} and {{ill|Quercus acutissima|ja|クヌギ}}. The area is designated as the Nagaoka Green Conservation Area by Tochigi Prefecture{{Cite web |url=https://www.pref.tochigi.lg.jp/d04/30jikannti/10nagaoka.html|title=10 Nagaoka Green Conservation Area|website=Tochigi Prefecture |accessdate=2024-11-11}}. Urbanization has increased in the surrounding area with the development of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}. == other_designation == === Tochigi Prefecture Designated other_designation === * Historic Sites ** Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun - Designated on July 26, 1955. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yomiuri Shimbun, Tochigi Edition, May 8, 2016, p. 27. == Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR West Exit): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} to the Teikyo University/Utsunomiya Museum of Art line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Toyosatodai Entrance" and walk for 20 minutes. ; By Car * From National Route 119 (Toyosato Bypass): ** Proceed north from the Nagaokamachi Intersection along the {{ill|Nagaoka Road|ja|長岡街道}} for 3 minutes. * From {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Route 63|ja|栃木県道63号藤原宇都宮線}}: ** Head west from the Sekibori-cho North Intersection via Nagaoka Road for 3 minutes. == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Nagaoka Hyakuana}} * {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}} * {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}} * {{ill|Taniguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}} * {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}} * {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} * {{ill|Hyakumoku|ja|百目鬼}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun}} [[Category:Rock-cut tombs]] [[Category:Ancient tombs in Tochigi Prefecture]] Eastern Cluster
[[File:Nagaoka Hyakuana, nishi-gun.jpg|280px]]
Western Cluster | location = {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Nagaokachō | coordinates = {{Coord|36|35|35|N|139|53|3|E}} | type = Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster | area = 52 tombs (44 in the Eastern Cluster, 8 in the Western Cluster) | built = Early 7th century | Buried Figure = | other_designation Designation = Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun) | Artifacts = }} '''Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun''' (長岡百穴古墳, also known as Nagaoka Hyakuana or the Nagaoka Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster) is a cluster of {{ill|rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}} located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is designated as a historic site by the Tochigi Prefecture. == Overview == The Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun is considered a family-oriented burial site, with tombs believed to have been constructed in the early 7th century. The cluster comprises 52 tombs, with 44 in the Eastern Cluster and 8 in the Western Cluster. Each tomb is approximately 1 meter in height and width and 2 meters in depth, carved into the sloped pumice tuff bedrock facing south. By modern times, the tombs were already exposed, with their original form lost. Artifacts that could have been buried with the deceased are no longer present, making detailed research challenging. However, the tomb designs, including some with side channels on the floors, indicate advanced craftsmanship characteristic of the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Many of the tomb walls feature carvings of {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩}} and {{ill|Hayagriva (Buddhism)|lt=Bato Kannon|ja|馬頭観音}}, believed to date back to the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} through the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. This suggests prolonged exposure to the elements and considerable cultural use, as evidenced by the inclusion of the site in local folklore, such as the {{ill|Hyakumoku Densetsu (One Hundred Eyes Legend)|ja|百目鬼伝説}}. File:Nagaoka hyakuana inside.jpg|Interior view File:Nagaoka hyakuana monument.jpg|Poetic inscription by {{ill|Misai Kosugi|ja|小杉放庵}} == Surrounding Environment == The Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun is situated in the northern part of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, at the southwestern foothills of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}, near {{ill|Tomatsuriyama|ja|戸祭山}}. The site is located on a plateau flanked by the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川}} and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川}}. The surrounding area features several other tumuli and ancient sites, including the {{ill|Taniguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, as well as remains from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. These sites are linked by the {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}}. The tombs are surrounded by greenery, including {{ill|Quercus serrata|ja|コナラ}} and {{ill|Quercus acutissima|ja|クヌギ}}. The area is designated as the Nagaoka Green Conservation Area by Tochigi Prefecture{{Cite web |url=https://www.pref.tochigi.lg.jp/d04/30jikannti/10nagaoka.html|title=10 Nagaoka Green Conservation Area|website=Tochigi Prefecture |accessdate=2024-11-11}}. Urbanization has increased in the surrounding area with the development of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}. == other_designation == === Tochigi Prefecture Designated other_designation === * Historic Sites ** Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun - Designated on July 26, 1955. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yomiuri Shimbun, Tochigi Edition, May 8, 2016, p. 27. == Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR West Exit): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} to the Teikyo University/Utsunomiya Museum of Art line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Toyosatodai Entrance" and walk for 20 minutes. ; By Car * From National Route 119 (Toyosato Bypass): ** Proceed north from the Nagaokamachi Intersection along the {{ill|Nagaoka Road|ja|長岡街道}} for 3 minutes. * From {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Route 63|ja|栃木県道63号藤原宇都宮線}}: ** Head west from the Sekibori-cho North Intersection via Nagaoka Road for 3 minutes. == Related Articles == Eastern Cluster
[[File:Nagaoka Hyakuana, nishi-gun.jpg|280px]]
Western Cluster | location = {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Nagaokachō | coordinates = {{Coord|36|35|35|N|139|53|3|E}} | type = Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster | area = 52 tombs (44 in the Eastern Cluster, 8 in the Western Cluster) | built = Early 7th century | Buried Figure = | other_designation Designation = Tochigi Prefecture Designated other_designation (Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun) | Artifacts = }} '''Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun''' (長岡百穴古墳, also known as Nagaoka Hyakuana or the Nagaoka Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster) is a cluster of {{ill|rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}} located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is designated as a historic site by the Tochigi Prefecture. == Overview == The Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun is considered a family-oriented burial site, with tombs believed to have been constructed in the early 7th century. The cluster comprises 52 tombs, with 44 in the Eastern Cluster and 8 in the Western Cluster. Each tomb is approximately 1 meter in height and width and 2 meters in depth, carved into the sloped pumice tuff bedrock facing south. By modern times, the tombs were already exposed, with their original form lost. Artifacts that could have been buried with the deceased are no longer present, making detailed research challenging. However, the tomb designs, including some with side channels on the floors, indicate advanced craftsmanship characteristic of the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Many of the tomb walls feature carvings of {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩}} and {{ill|Hayagriva (Buddhism)|lt=Bato Kannon|ja|馬頭観音}}, believed to date back to the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} through the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. This suggests prolonged exposure to the elements and considerable cultural use, as evidenced by the inclusion of the site in local folklore, such as the {{ill|Hyakumoku Densetsu (One Hundred Eyes Legend)|ja|百目鬼伝説}}. File:Nagaoka hyakuana inside.jpg|Interior view File:Nagaoka hyakuana monument.jpg|Poetic inscription by {{ill|Misai Kosugi|ja|小杉放庵}} == Surrounding Environment == The Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun is situated in the northern part of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, at the southwestern foothills of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}, near {{ill|Tomatsuriyama|ja|戸祭山}}. The site is located on a plateau flanked by the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川}} and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川}}. The surrounding area features several other tumuli and ancient sites, including the {{ill|Taniguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, as well as remains from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. These sites are linked by the {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}}. The tombs are surrounded by greenery, including {{ill|Quercus serrata|ja|コナラ}} and {{ill|Quercus acutissima|ja|クヌギ}}. The area is designated as the Nagaoka Green Conservation Area by Tochigi Prefecture{{Cite web |url=https://www.pref.tochigi.lg.jp/d04/30jikannti/10nagaoka.html|title=10 Nagaoka Green Conservation Area|website=Tochigi Prefecture |accessdate=2024-11-11}}. Urbanization has increased in the surrounding area with the development of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}. == other_designation == === Tochigi Prefecture Designated other_designation === * other_designations ** Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun - Designated on July 26, 1955. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yomiuri Shimbun, Tochigi Edition, May 8, 2016, p. 27. == Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR West Exit): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} to the Teikyo University/Utsunomiya Museum of Art line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Toyosatodai Entrance" and walk for 20 minutes. ; By Car * From National Route 119 (Toyosato Bypass): ** Proceed north from the Nagaokamachi Intersection along the {{ill|Nagaoka Road|ja|長岡街道}} for 3 minutes. * From {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Route 63|ja|栃木県道63号藤原宇都宮線}}: ** Head west from the Sekibori-cho North Intersection via Nagaoka Road for 3 minutes. == Related Articles == Eastern Cluster
[[File:Nagaoka Hyakuana, nishi-gun.jpg|280px]]
Western Cluster | location = {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Nagaokachō | coordinates = {{Coord|36|35|35|N|139|53|3|E}} | type = Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster | area = 52 tombs (44 in the Eastern Cluster, 8 in the Western Cluster) | built = Early 7th century | Buried Figure = | other_designation Designation = Tochigi Prefecture Designated other_designation (Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun) | burial goods = }} '''Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun''' (長岡百穴古墳, also known as Nagaoka Hyakuana or the Nagaoka Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster) is a cluster of {{ill|rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}} located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is designated as a historic site by the Tochigi Prefecture. == Overview == The Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun is considered a family-oriented burial site, with tombs believed to have been constructed in the early 7th century. The cluster comprises 52 tombs, with 44 in the Eastern Cluster and 8 in the Western Cluster. Each tomb is approximately 1 meter in height and width and 2 meters in depth, carved into the sloped pumice tuff bedrock facing south. By modern times, the tombs were already exposed, with their original form lost. burial goods that could have been buried with the deceased are no longer present, making detailed research challenging. However, the tomb designs, including some with side channels on the floors, indicate advanced craftsmanship characteristic of the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Many of the tomb walls feature carvings of {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩}} and {{ill|Hayagriva (Buddhism)|lt=Bato Kannon|ja|馬頭観音}}, believed to date back to the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} through the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. This suggests prolonged exposure to the elements and considerable cultural use, as evidenced by the inclusion of the site in local folklore, such as the {{ill|Hyakumoku Densetsu (One Hundred Eyes Legend)|ja|百目鬼伝説}}. File:Nagaoka hyakuana inside.jpg|Interior view File:Nagaoka hyakuana monument.jpg|Poetic inscription by {{ill|Misai Kosugi|ja|小杉放庵}} == Surrounding Environment == The Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun is situated in the northern part of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, at the southwestern foothills of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}, near {{ill|Tomatsuriyama|ja|戸祭山}}. The site is located on a plateau flanked by the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川}} and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川}}. The surrounding area features several other tumuli and ancient sites, including the {{ill|Taniguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, as well as remains from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. These sites are linked by the {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}}. The tombs are surrounded by greenery, including {{ill|Quercus serrata|ja|コナラ}} and {{ill|Quercus acutissima|ja|クヌギ}}. The area is designated as the Nagaoka Green Conservation Area by Tochigi Prefecture{{Cite web |url=https://www.pref.tochigi.lg.jp/d04/30jikannti/10nagaoka.html|title=10 Nagaoka Green Conservation Area|website=Tochigi Prefecture |accessdate=2024-11-11}}. Urbanization has increased in the surrounding area with the development of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}. == other_designation == === Tochigi Prefecture Designated other_designation === * other_designations ** Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun - Designated on July 26, 1955. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yomiuri Shimbun, Tochigi Edition, May 8, 2016, p. 27. == Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR West Exit): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} to the Teikyo University/Utsunomiya Museum of Art line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Toyosatodai Entrance" and walk for 20 minutes. ; By Car * From National Route 119 (Toyosato Bypass): ** Proceed north from the Nagaokamachi Intersection along the {{ill|Nagaoka Road|ja|長岡街道}} for 3 minutes. * From {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Route 63|ja|栃木県道63号藤原宇都宮線}}: ** Head west from the Sekibori-cho North Intersection via Nagaoka Road for 3 minutes. == Related Articles == revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun | image = [[File:Nagaoka Hyakuana, higashi-gun-2.jpg|280px]]
Eastern Cluster
[[File:Nagaoka Hyakuana, nishi-gun.jpg|280px]]
Western Cluster | location = {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Nagaokachō | coordinates = {{Coord|36|35|35|N|139|53|3|E}} | type = Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster | area = 52 tombs (44 in the Eastern Cluster, 8 in the Western Cluster) | built = Early 7th century | Buried Figure = | other_designation Designation = Tochigi Prefecture Designated other_designation (Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun) | burial goods = }} '''Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun''' (長岡百穴古墳, also known as Nagaoka Hyakuana or the Nagaoka Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster) is a cluster of {{ill|rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}} located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is designation1 as a historic site by the Tochigi Prefecture. == Overview == The Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun is considered a family-oriented burial site, with tombs believed to have been constructed in the early 7th century. The cluster comprises 52 tombs, with 44 in the Eastern Cluster and 8 in the Western Cluster. Each tomb is approximately 1 meter in height and width and 2 meters in depth, carved into the sloped pumice tuff bedrock facing south. By modern times, the tombs were already exposed, with their original form lost. burial goods that could have been buried with the deceased are no longer present, making detailed research challenging. However, the tomb designs, including some with side channels on the floors, indicate advanced craftsmanship characteristic of the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Many of the tomb walls feature carvings of {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩}} and {{ill|Hayagriva (Buddhism)|lt=Bato Kannon|ja|馬頭観音}}, believed to date back to the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} through the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. This suggests prolonged exposure to the elements and considerable cultural use, as evidenced by the inclusion of the site in local folklore, such as the {{ill|Hyakumoku Densetsu (One Hundred Eyes Legend)|ja|百目鬼伝説}}. File:Nagaoka hyakuana inside.jpg|Interior view File:Nagaoka hyakuana monument.jpg|Poetic inscription by {{ill|Misai Kosugi|ja|小杉放庵}} == Surrounding Environment == The Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun is situated in the northern part of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, at the southwestern foothills of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}, near {{ill|Tomatsuriyama|ja|戸祭山}}. The site is located on a plateau flanked by the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川}} and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川}}. The surrounding area features several other tumuli and ancient sites, including the {{ill|Taniguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, as well as remains from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. These sites are linked by the {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}}. The tombs are surrounded by greenery, including {{ill|Quercus serrata|ja|コナラ}} and {{ill|Quercus acutissima|ja|クヌギ}}. The area is designation1 as the Nagaoka Green Conservation Area by Tochigi Prefecture{{Cite web |url=https://www.pref.tochigi.lg.jp/d04/30jikannti/10nagaoka.html|title=10 Nagaoka Green Conservation Area|website=Tochigi Prefecture |accessdate=2024-11-11}}. Urbanization has increased in the surrounding area with the development of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}. == other_designation == === Tochigi Prefecture Designated other_designation === * other_designations ** Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun - Designated on July 26, 1955. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yomiuri Shimbun, Tochigi Edition, May 8, 2016, p. 27. == Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR West Exit): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} to the Teikyo University/Utsunomiya Museum of Art line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Toyosatodai Entrance" and walk for 20 minutes. ; By Car * From National Route 119 (Toyosato Bypass): ** Proceed north from the Nagaokamachi Intersection along the {{ill|Nagaoka Road|ja|長岡街道}} for 3 minutes. * From {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Route 63|ja|栃木県道63号藤原宇都宮線}}: ** Head west from the Sekibori-cho North Intersection via Nagaoka Road for 3 minutes. == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Nagaoka Hyakuana}} * {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}} * {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}} * {{ill|Taniguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}} * {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}} * {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} * {{ill|Hyakumoku|ja|百目鬼}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun}} [[Category:Rock-cut tombs]] [[Category:Ancient tombs in Tochigi Prefecture]] This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun | image = [[File:Nagaoka Hyakuana, higashi-gun-2.jpg|280px]]
Eastern Cluster
[[File:Nagaoka Hyakuana, nishi-gun.jpg|280px]]
Western Cluster | location = {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Nagaokachō | coordinates = {{Coord|36|35|35|N|139|53|3|E}} | type = Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster | area = 52 tombs (44 in the Eastern Cluster, 8 in the Western Cluster) | built = Early 7th century | Buried Figure = | other_other_designation Designation = Tochigi Prefecture Designated other_other_designation (Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun) | burial goods = }} '''Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun''' (長岡百穴古墳, also known as Nagaoka Hyakuana or the Nagaoka Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster) is a cluster of {{ill|rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}} located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is other_designation1 as a historic site by the Tochigi Prefecture. == Overview == The Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun is considered a family-oriented burial site, with tombs believed to have been constructed in the early 7th century. The cluster comprises 52 tombs, with 44 in the Eastern Cluster and 8 in the Western Cluster. Each tomb is approximately 1 meter in height and width and 2 meters in depth, carved into the sloped pumice tuff bedrock facing south. By modern times, the tombs were already exposed, with their original type lost. burial goods that could have been buried with the deceased are no longer present, making detailed research challenging. However, the tomb designs, including some with side channels on the floors, indicate advanced craftsmanship characteristic of the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Many of the tomb walls feature carvings of {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩}} and {{ill|Hayagriva (Buddhism)|lt=Bato Kannon|ja|馬頭観音}}, believed to date back to the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} through the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. This suggests prolonged exposure to the elements and considerable cultural use, as evidenced by the inclusion of the site in local folklore, such as the {{ill|Hyakumoku Densetsu (One Hundred Eyes Legend)|ja|百目鬼伝説}}. File:Nagaoka hyakuana inside.jpg|Interior view File:Nagaoka hyakuana monument.jpg|Poetic inscription by {{ill|Misai Kosugi|ja|小杉放庵}} == Surrounding Environment == The Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun is situated in the northern part of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, at the southwestern foothills of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}, near {{ill|Tomatsuriyama|ja|戸祭山}}. The site is located on a plateau flanked by the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川}} and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川}}. The surrounding area features several other tumuli and ancient sites, including the {{ill|Taniguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, as well as remains from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. These sites are linked by the {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}}. The tombs are surrounded by greenery, including {{ill|Quercus serrata|ja|コナラ}} and {{ill|Quercus acutissima|ja|クヌギ}}. The area is other_designation1 as the Nagaoka Green Conservation Area by Tochigi Prefecture{{Cite web |url=https://www.pref.tochigi.lg.jp/d04/30jikannti/10nagaoka.html|title=10 Nagaoka Green Conservation Area|website=Tochigi Prefecture |accessdate=2024-11-11}}. Urbanization has increased in the surrounding area with the development of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}. == other_other_designation == === Tochigi Prefecture Designated other_other_designation === * other_other_designations ** Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun - Designated on July 26, 1955. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yomiuri Shimbun, Tochigi Edition, May 8, 2016, p. 27. == Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR West Exit): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} to the Teikyo University/Utsunomiya Museum of Art line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Toyosatodai Entrance" and walk for 20 minutes. ; By Car * From National Route 119 (Toyosato Bypass): ** Proceed north from the Nagaokamachi Intersection along the {{ill|Nagaoka Road|ja|長岡街道}} for 3 minutes. * From {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Route 63|ja|栃木県道63号藤原宇都宮線}}: ** Head west from the Sekibori-cho North Intersection via Nagaoka Road for 3 minutes. == Related Articles == {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun | image = [[File:Nagaoka Hyakuana, higashi-gun-2.jpg|280px]]
Eastern Cluster
[[File:Nagaoka Hyakuana, nishi-gun.jpg|280px]]
Western Cluster | location = {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Nagaokachō | coordinates = {{Coord|36|35|35|N|139|53|3|E}} | type = Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster | area = 52 tombs (44 in the Eastern Cluster, 8 in the Western Cluster) | built = Early 7th century | Buried Figure = | other_designation Designation = Tochigi Prefecture Designated other_designation (Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun) | burial goods = }} '''Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun''' (長岡百穴古墳, also known as Nagaoka Hyakuana or the Nagaoka Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster) is a cluster of {{ill|rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}} located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is other_designation1 as a historic site by the Tochigi Prefecture. == Overview == The Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun is considered a family-oriented burial site, with tombs believed to have been constructed in the early 7th century. The cluster comprises 52 tombs, with 44 in the Eastern Cluster and 8 in the Western Cluster. Each tomb is approximately 1 meter in height and width and 2 meters in depth, carved into the sloped pumice tuff bedrock facing south. By modern times, the tombs were already exposed, with their original type lost. burial goods that could have been buried with the deceased are no longer present, making detailed research challenging. However, the tomb designs, including some with side channels on the floors, indicate advanced craftsmanship characteristic of the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Many of the tomb walls feature carvings of {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩}} and {{ill|Hayagriva (Buddhism)|lt=Bato Kannon|ja|馬頭観音}}, believed to date back to the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} through the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. This suggests prolonged exposure to the elements and considerable cultural use, as evidenced by the inclusion of the site in local folklore, such as the {{ill|Hyakumoku Densetsu (One Hundred Eyes Legend)|ja|百目鬼伝説}}. File:Nagaoka hyakuana inside.jpg|Interior view File:Nagaoka hyakuana monument.jpg|Poetic inscription by {{ill|Misai Kosugi|ja|小杉放庵}} == Surrounding Environment == The Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun is situated in the northern part of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, at the southwestern foothills of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}, near {{ill|Tomatsuriyama|ja|戸祭山}}. The site is located on a plateau flanked by the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川}} and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川}}. The surrounding area features several other tumuli and ancient sites, including the {{ill|Taniguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, as well as remains from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. These sites are linked by the {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}}. The tombs are surrounded by greenery, including {{ill|Quercus serrata|ja|コナラ}} and {{ill|Quercus acutissima|ja|クヌギ}}. The area is other_designation1 as the Nagaoka Green Conservation Area by Tochigi Prefecture{{Cite web |url=https://www.pref.tochigi.lg.jp/d04/30jikannti/10nagaoka.html|title=10 Nagaoka Green Conservation Area|website=Tochigi Prefecture |accessdate=2024-11-11}}. Urbanization has increased in the surrounding area with the development of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}. == other_designation == === Tochigi Prefecture Designated other_designation === * other_designations ** Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun - Designated on July 26, 1955. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yomiuri Shimbun, Tochigi Edition, May 8, 2016, p. 27. == Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR West Exit): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} to the Teikyo University/Utsunomiya Museum of Art line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Toyosatodai Entrance" and walk for 20 minutes. ; By Car * From National Route 119 (Toyosato Bypass): ** Proceed north from the Nagaokamachi Intersection along the {{ill|Nagaoka Road|ja|長岡街道}} for 3 minutes. * From {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Route 63|ja|栃木県道63号藤原宇都宮線}}: ** Head west from the Sekibori-cho North Intersection via Nagaoka Road for 3 minutes. == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Nagaoka Hyakuana}} * {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}} * {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}} * {{ill|Taniguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}} * {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}} So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun | image = [[File:Nagaoka Hyakuana, higashi-gun-2.jpg|280px]]
Eastern Cluster
[[File:Nagaoka Hyakuana, nishi-gun.jpg|280px]]
Western Cluster | location = {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Nagaokachō | coordinates = {{Coord|36|35|35|N|139|53|3|E}} | type = Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster | area = 52 tombs (44 in the Eastern Cluster, 8 in the Western Cluster) | built = Early 7th century | Buried Figure = | other_designation Designation = Tochigi Prefecture Designated other_designation (Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun) | burial goods = }} '''Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun''' (長岡百穴古墳, also known as Nagaoka Hyakuana or the Nagaoka Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster) is a cluster of {{ill|rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}} located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is other_designation1 as a historic site by the Tochigi Prefecture. == Overview == The Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun is considered a family-oriented burial site, with tombs believed to have been constructed in the early 7th century. The cluster comprises 52 tombs, with 44 in the Eastern Cluster and 8 in the Western Cluster. Each tomb is approximately 1 meter in height and width and 2 meters in depth, carved into the sloped pumice tuff bedrock facing south. By modern times, the tombs were already exposed, with their original type lost. burial goods that could have been buried with the deceased are no longer present, making detailed research challenging. However, the tomb designs, including some with side channels on the floors, indicate advanced craftsmanship characteristic of the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Many of the tomb walls feature carvings of {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩}} and {{ill|Hayagriva (Buddhism)|lt=Bato Kannon|ja|馬頭観音}}, believed to date back to the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} through the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. This suggests prolonged exposure to the elements and considerable cultural use, as evidenced by the inclusion of the site in local folklore, such as the {{ill|Hyakumoku Densetsu (One Hundred Eyes Legend)|ja|百目鬼伝説}}. File:Nagaoka hyakuana inside.jpg|Interior view File:Nagaoka hyakuana monument.jpg|Poetic inscription by {{ill|Misai Kosugi|ja|小杉放庵}} == Surrounding Environment == The Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun is situated in the northern part of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, at the southwestern foothills of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}, near {{ill|Tomatsuriyama|ja|戸祭山}}. The site is located on a plateau flanked by the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川}} and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川}}. The surrounding area features several other tumuli and ancient sites, including the {{ill|Taniguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, as well as remains from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. These sites are linked by the {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}}. The tombs are surrounded by greenery, including {{ill|Quercus serrata|ja|コナラ}} and {{ill|Quercus acutissima|ja|クヌギ}}. The area is other_designation1 as the Nagaoka Green Conservation Area by Tochigi Prefecture{{Cite web |url=https://www.pref.tochigi.lg.jp/d04/30jikannti/10nagaoka.html|title=10 Nagaoka Green Conservation Area|website=Tochigi Prefecture |accessdate=2024-11-11}}. Urbanization has increased in the surrounding area with the development of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}. == other_designation == === Tochigi Prefecture Designated other_designation === * other_designations ** Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun - Designated on July 26, 1955. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yomiuri Shimbun, Tochigi Edition, May 8, 2016, p. 27. == Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR West Exit): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} to the Teikyo University/Utsunomiya Museum of Art line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Toyosatodai Entrance" and walk for 20 minutes. ; By Car * From National Route 119 (Toyosato Bypass): ** Proceed north from the Nagaokamachi Intersection along the {{ill|Nagaoka Road|ja|長岡街道}} for 3 minutes. * From {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Route 63|ja|栃木県道63号藤原宇都宮線}}: ** Head west from the Sekibori-cho North Intersection via Nagaoka Road for 3 minutes. == Related Articles == Urbanization has increased in the surrounding area with the development of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}. == other_designation == === Tochigi Prefecture Designated other_designation === * other_designations ** Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun - Designated on July 26, 1955. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yomiuri Shimbun, Tochigi Edition, May 8, 2016, p. 27. == Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR West Exit): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} to the Teikyo University/Utsunomiya Museum of Art line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Toyosatodai Entrance" and walk for 20 minutes. ; By Car * From National Route 119 (Toyosato Bypass): ** Proceed north from the Nagaokamachi Intersection along the {{ill|Nagaoka revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun | image = [[File:Nagaoka Hyakuana, higashi-gun-2.jpg|280px]]
Eastern Cluster
[[File:Nagaoka Hyakuana, nishi-gun.jpg|280px]]
Western Cluster | location = {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Nagaokachō | coordinates = {{Coord|36|35|35|N|139|53|3|E}} | type = Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster | area = 52 tombs (44 in the Eastern Cluster, 8 in the Western Cluster) | built = Early 7th century | Buried Figure = | other_designation Designation = Tochigi Prefecture Designated other_designation (Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun) | burial goods = }} '''Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun''' (長岡百穴古墳, also known as Nagaoka Hyakuana or the Nagaoka Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster) is a cluster of {{ill|rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}} located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is other_designation1 as a historic site by the Tochigi Prefecture. == Overview == The Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun is considered a family-oriented burial site, with tombs believed to have been constructed in the early 7th century. The cluster comprises 52 tombs, with 44 in the Eastern Cluster and 8 in the Western Cluster. Each tomb is approximately 1 meter in height and width and 2 meters in depth, carved into the sloped pumice tuff bedrock facing south. By modern times, the tombs were already exposed, with their original type lost. burial goods that could have been buried with the deceased are no longer present, making detailed research challenging. However, the tomb designs, including some with side channels on the floors, indicate advanced craftsmanship characteristic of the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Many of the tomb walls feature carvings of {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩}} and {{ill|Hayagriva (Buddhism)|lt=Bato Kannon|ja|馬頭観音}}, believed to date back to the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} through the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. This suggests prolonged exposure to the elements and considerable cultural use, as evidenced by the inclusion of the site in local folklore, such as the {{ill|Hyakumoku Densetsu (One Hundred Eyes Legend)|ja|百目鬼伝説}}. File:Nagaoka hyakuana inside.jpg|Interior view File:Nagaoka hyakuana monument.jpg|Poetic inscription by {{ill|Misai Kosugi|ja|小杉放庵}} == Surrounding Environment == The Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun is situated in the northern part of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, at the southwestern foothills of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}, near {{ill|Tomatsuriyama|ja|戸祭山}}. The site is located on a plateau flanked by the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川}} and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川}}. The surrounding area features several other tumuli and ancient sites, including the {{ill|Taniguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, as well as remains from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. These sites are linked by the {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}}. The tombs are surrounded by greenery, including {{ill|Quercus serrata|ja|コナラ}} and {{ill|Quercus acutissima|ja|クヌギ}}. The area is other_designation1 as the Nagaoka Green Conservation Area by Tochigi Prefecture{{Cite web |url=https://www.pref.tochigi.lg.jp/d04/30jikannti/10nagaoka.html|title=10 Nagaoka Green Conservation Area|website=Tochigi Prefecture |accessdate=2024-11-11}}. Urbanization has increased in the surrounding area with the development of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}. == other_designation == === Tochigi Prefecture Designated other_designation === * other_designations ** Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun - Designated on July 26, 1955. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yomiuri Shimbun, Tochigi Edition, May 8, 2016, p. 27. == Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR West Exit): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} to the Teikyo University/Utsunomiya Museum of Art line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Toyosatodai Entrance" and walk for 20 minutes. ; By Car * From National Route 119 (Toyosato Bypass): ** Proceed north from the Nagaokamachi Intersection along the {{ill|Nagaoka Road|ja|長岡街道}} for 3 minutes. * From {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Route 63|ja|栃木県道63号藤原宇都宮線}}: ** Head west from the Sekibori-cho North Intersection via Nagaoka Road for 3 minutes. == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Nagaoka Hyakuana}} * {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}} * {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}} * {{ill|Taniguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}} * {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}} * {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} * {{ill|Hyakumoku|ja|百目鬼}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun}} [[Category:Rock-cut tombs]] [[Category:Ancient tombs in Tochigi Prefecture]] revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun | image = [[File:Nagaoka Hyakuana, higashi-gun-2.jpg|280px]]
Eastern Cluster
[[File:Nagaoka Hyakuana, nishi-gun.jpg|280px]]
Western Cluster | location = {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Nagaokachō | coordinates = {{Coord|36|35|35|N|139|53|3|E}} | type = Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster | area = 52 tombs (44 in the Eastern Cluster, 8 in the Western Cluster) | built = Early 7th century | Buried Figure = | other_designation Designation = Tochigi Prefecture Designated other_designation (Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun) | burial goods = }} '''Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun''' (長岡百穴古墳, also known as Nagaoka Hyakuana or the Nagaoka Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster) is a cluster of {{ill|rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}} located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is other_designation1 as a historic site by the Tochigi Prefecture. == Overview == The Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun is considered a family-oriented burial site, with tombs believed to have been constructed in the early 7th century. The cluster comprises 52 tombs, with 44 in the Eastern Cluster and 8 in the Western Cluster. Each tomb is approximately 1 meter in height and width and 2 meters in depth, carved into the sloped pumice tuff bedrock facing south. By modern times, the tombs were already exposed, with their original type lost. burial goods that could have been buried with the deceased are no longer present, making detailed research challenging. However, the tomb designs, including some with side channels on the floors, indicate advanced craftsmanship characteristic of the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Many of the tomb walls feature carvings of {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩}} and {{ill|Hayagriva (Buddhism)|lt=Bato Kannon|ja|馬頭観音}}, believed to date back to the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} through the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. This suggests prolonged exposure to the elements and considerable cultural use, as evidenced by the inclusion of the site in local folklore, such as the {{ill|Hyakumoku Densetsu (One Hundred Eyes Legend)|ja|百目鬼伝説}}. File:Nagaoka hyakuana inside.jpg|Interior view File:Nagaoka hyakuana monument.jpg|Poetic inscription by {{ill|Misai Kosugi|ja|小杉放庵}} == Surrounding Environment == The Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun is situated in the northern part of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, at the southwestern foothills of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}, near {{ill|Tomatsuriyama|ja|戸祭山}}. The site is located on a plateau flanked by the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川}} and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川}}. The surrounding area features several other tumuli and ancient sites, including the {{ill|Taniguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, as well as remains from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. These sites are linked by the {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}}. The tombs are surrounded by greenery, including {{ill|Quercus serrata|ja|コナラ}} and {{ill|Quercus acutissima|ja|クヌギ}}. The area is other_designation1 as the Nagaoka Green Conservation Area by Tochigi Prefecture{{Cite web |url=https://www.pref.tochigi.lg.jp/d04/30jikannti/10nagaoka.html|title=10 Nagaoka Green Conservation Area|website=Tochigi Prefecture |accessdate=2024-11-11}}. Urbanization has increased in the surrounding area with the development of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}. == other_designation == === Tochigi Prefecture Designated other_designation === * other_designations ** Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun - Designated on July 26, 1955. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yomiuri Shimbun, Tochigi Edition, May 8, 2016, p. 27. == Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR West Exit): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} to the Teikyo University/Utsunomiya Museum of Art line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Toyosatodai Entrance" and walk for 20 minutes. ; By Car * From National Route 119 (Toyosato Bypass): ** Proceed north from the Nagaokamachi Intersection along the {{ill|Nagaoka Road|ja|長岡街道}} for 3 minutes. * From {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Route 63|ja|栃木県道63号藤原宇都宮線}}: ** Head west from the Sekibori-cho North Intersection via Nagaoka Road for 3 minutes. == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Nagaoka Hyakuana}} * {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}} * {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}} * {{ill|Taniguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}} * {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}} * {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} * {{ill|Hyakumoku|ja|百目鬼}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun}} [[Category:Rock-cut tombs]] [[Category:Ancient tombs in Tochigi Prefecture]] The site is located on a plateau flanked by the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川}} and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川}}. The surrounding area features several other tumuli and ancient sites, including the {{ill|Taniguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, as well as remains from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. These sites are linked by the {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}}. The tombs are surrounded by greenery, including {{ill|Quercus serrata|ja|コナラ}} and {{ill|Quercus acutissima|ja|クヌギ}}. The area is other_designation1 as the Nagaoka Green Conservation Area by Tochigi Prefecture{{Cite web |url=https://www.pref.tochigi.lg.jp/d04/30jikannti/10nagaoka.html|title=10 Nagaoka Green Conservation Area|website=Tochigi Prefecture |accessdate=2024-11-11}}. Urbanization has increased in the surrounding area with the development of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}. == other_designation == === Tochigi Prefecture Designated other_designation === * other_designations ** Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun - Designated on July 26, 1955. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yomiuri Shimbun, Tochigi Edition, May 8, 2016, p. 27. == Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR West Exit): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} to the Teikyo University/Utsunomiya Museum of Art line (approx. 15 minutes).
