The site is located on a plateau flanked by the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川}} and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川}}. The surrounding area features several other tumuli and ancient sites, including the {{ill|Taniguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, as well as remains from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. These sites are linked by the {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}}. The tombs are surrounded by greenery, including {{ill|Quercus serrata|ja|コナラ}} and {{ill|Quercus acutissima|ja|クヌギ}}. The area is Historic Site1 as the Nagaoka Green Conservation Area by Tochigi Prefecture{{Cite web |url=https://www.pref.tochigi.lg.jp/d04/30jikannti/10nagaoka.html|title=10 Nagaoka Green Conservation Area|website=Tochigi Prefecture |accessdate=2024-11-11}}. Urbanization has increased in the surrounding area with the development of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}. == Historic Site == === Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Sites ** Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun - Designated on July 26, 1955. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yomiuri Shimbun, Tochigi Edition, May 8, 2016, p. 27. == Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR West Exit): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} to the Teikyo University/Utsunomiya Museum of Art line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Toyosatodai Entrance" and walk for 20 minutes. ; By Car * From National Route 119 (Toyosato Bypass): ** Proceed north from the Nagaokamachi Intersection along the {{ill|Nagaoka Road|ja|長岡街道}} for 3 minutes. * From {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Route 63|ja|栃木県道63号藤原宇都宮線}}: ** Head west from the Sekibori-cho North Intersection via Nagaoka Road for 3 minutes. == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Nagaoka Hyakuana}} * {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}} * {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}} * {{ill|Taniguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}} * {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}} * {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} * {{ill|Hyakumoku|ja|百目鬼}} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun}} {{citation needed|Nagaoka Hyakka Kofun}} {{citation needed|Nagaoka Hundred Caves}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Rock-cut tombs]] [[Category:Ancient tombs in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Utsunomiya]] [[Category:Tochigi Prefecture-Historic Site1 historic sites]] [[Category:Utsunomiya Scenic Spots]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Nagaoka Hyakka Kofun]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagaoka Hundred Caves]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagaoka Hundred Caves]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Nagaoka Hundred Caves]] [[sn:Nagaoka Hyakka Kofun]] [[ja:%E9%95%B7%E5%B2%A1%E7%99%BE%E7%A9%B4%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[en:Nagaoka Hyakka Kofun]] [[ja:&action=edit]]

revision.comment: 13 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:長岡百穴古墳 up to rev 102535258
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳| |名称=長岡百穴古墳 |画像=[[File:Nagaoka Hyakuana, higashi-gun-2.jpg|280px]]
東群
[[File:Nagaoka Hyakuana, nishi-gun.jpg|280px]]
西群 |所在地=[[栃木県]][[宇都宮市]]長岡町 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|35|35|N|139|53|3|E|}} |形状=横穴墓群 |規模=52基（東群44基・西群8基） |築造年代=7世紀前半頃 |被葬者= |史跡指定=栃木県指定史跡「長岡百穴古墳」 |出土品= }} '''長岡百穴古墳'''（ながおかひゃくあなこふん、長岡百穴/長岡横穴墓群）は、[[栃木県]][[宇都宮市]]長岡町にある[[横穴墓]]群。栃木県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 概要 == 長岡百穴古墳は7世紀前期頃に造成された家族墓的な要素が強い横穴墓群と考えられている。東群44基と西群8基の計52基の横穴（縦横各1メートル、奥行き約2メートル程度）が南面する[[軽石]][[凝灰岩]]の傾斜面に開口している。近代では既に横穴が露出して原型を留めておらず、埋葬品に基づく考証は不能であるが、床面に側溝を有する穴も散見されるなど、比較的成熟した技術をもって掘削された墓穴とみられることから、[[古墳時代]]末期のものと推定されている。ほぼすべての穴の壁面に[[室町時代]]から[[江戸時代]]にかけて彫られたとみられる[[地蔵菩薩]]像や[[馬頭観音]]像が見られることから、外気に曝されていた期間が非常に長いものと窺われ、その為か欠損も多く保存状態はあまり良くない。宇都宮に伝わる「[[百目鬼|百目鬼伝説]]」にも登場することから、本伝説が成立した頃には既に百穴古墳の存在が知られていたものと推察される。 ファイル:Nagaoka hyakuana inside.jpg|内部 ファイル:Nagaoka hyakuana monument.jpg|[[小杉放庵]]の歌碑 == 周辺環境 == 長岡百穴古墳は[[宇都宮市]]北部の[[宇都宮丘陵]]の南部、[[戸祭山]]の北西麓で[[田川 (利根川水系)|田川]]と[[釜川]]に挟まれた台地上に立地する。周辺には当墳をはじめ、[[谷口山古墳]]や[[北山古墳群]]、[[瓦塚古墳群]]、[[戸祭大塚古墳]]などの古代古墳群、また[[奈良時代]]の遺跡である[[水道山瓦窯跡]]などの古代遺跡が数多く認められており、[[豊郷まほろばの道]]が整備されている。 長岡百穴古墳群の周囲は[[コナラ]]や[[クヌギ]]等に囲まれた緑地となっており、栃木県により長岡緑地環境保全地域に指定されている{{Cite web |url=https://www.pref.tochigi.lg.jp/d04/30jikannti/10nagaoka.html|title=10 長岡（ながおか）緑地環境保全地域|website=栃木県 |accessdate=2024-11-11}}。 一方、[[宇都宮環状道路]]が開通したことで近隣では宅地化も進んでいる。 == 文化財 == === 栃木県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 長岡百穴古墳 - 1955年（昭和30年）7月26日指定。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 読売新聞栃木版 2016年5月8日 27面掲載。 == アクセス == ;路線バス * [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[宇都宮駅]]西口から ** [[関東自動車 (栃木県)|関東バス]]　帝京大学・宇都宮美術館線に乗車約15分、「[[豊郷台]]入口」下車徒歩20分。 ;車 * [[国道119号]]（[[宇都宮環状道路|豊郷バイパス]]）長岡町交差点から ** [[栃木県道157号下岡本上戸祭線|長岡街道]]を北進、3分。 * [[栃木県道63号藤原宇都宮線|田原街道]]関堀町北交差点から ** 長岡街道を西進、3分。 == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Nagaoka Hyakuana}} * [[北山古墳群]] * [[瓦塚古墳群]] * [[谷口山古墳]] * [[戸祭大塚古墳]] * [[豊郷まほろばの道]] * [[百目鬼]] {{DEFAULTSORT:なかおかひやくあなこふん}} [[Category:横穴墓]] [[Category:栃木県の古墳]] [[Category:宇都宮市の歴史]] [[Category:栃木県指定史跡]] [[Category:うつのみや百景]]

{{afc comment|1=See [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun | image = [[File:Nagaoka Hyakuana, higashi-gun-2.jpg|280px]]
Eastern Cluster
[[File:Nagaoka Hyakuana, nishi-gun.jpg|280px]]
Western Cluster | location = {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Nagaokachō | coordinates = {{Coord|36|35|35|N|139|53|3|E}} | type = Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster | area = 52 tombs (44 in the Eastern Cluster, 8 in the Western Cluster) | built = Early 7th century | Interred_individual = | Historic Site designation1 = Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun) | burial goods = }} '''Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun''' (長岡百穴古墳, also known as Nagaoka Hyakuana or the Nagaoka Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster) is a cluster of {{ill|rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}} located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is Historic Site1 as a historic site by the Tochigi Prefecture. == Overview == The Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun is considered a family-oriented burial site, with tombs believed to have been constructed in the early 7th century. The cluster comprises 52 tombs, with 44 in the Eastern Cluster and 8 in the Western Cluster. Each tomb is approximately 1 meter in height and width and 2 meters in depth, carved into the sloped pumice tuff bedrock facing south. By modern times, the tombs were already exposed, with their original type lost. burial goods that could have been buried with the deceased are no longer present, making detailed research challenging. However, the tomb designs, including some with side channels on the floors, indicate advanced craftsmanship characteristic of the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Many of the tomb walls feature carvings of {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩}} and {{ill|Hayagriva (Buddhism)|lt=Bato Kannon|ja|馬頭観音}}, believed to date back to the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} through the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. This suggests prolonged exposure to the elements and considerable cultural use, as evidenced by the inclusion of the site in local folklore, such as the {{ill|Hyakumoku Densetsu (One Hundred Eyes Legend)|ja|百目鬼伝説}}. File:Nagaoka hyakuana inside.jpg|Interior view File:Nagaoka hyakuana monument.jpg|Poetic inscription by {{ill|Misai Kosugi|ja|小杉放庵}} == Surrounding Environment == The Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun is situated in the northern part of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, at the southwestern foothills of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}, near {{ill|Tomatsuriyama|ja|戸祭山}}. The site is located on a plateau flanked by the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川}} and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川}}. The surrounding area features several other tumuli and ancient sites, including the {{ill|Taniguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}}, and the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, as well as remains from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, such as the {{ill|Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}. These sites are linked by the {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}}. The tombs are surrounded by greenery, including {{ill|Quercus serrata|ja|コナラ}} and {{ill|Quercus acutissima|ja|クヌギ}}. The area is Historic Site1 as the Nagaoka Green Conservation Area by Tochigi Prefecture{{Cite web |url=https://www.pref.tochigi.lg.jp/d04/30jikannti/10nagaoka.html|title=10 Nagaoka Green Conservation Area|website=Tochigi Prefecture |accessdate=2024-11-11}}. Urbanization has increased in the surrounding area with the development of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}. == Historic Site == === Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Sites ** Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun - Designated on July 26, 1955. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yomiuri Shimbun, Tochigi Edition, May 8, 2016, p. 27. == Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR West Exit): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} to the Teikyo University/Utsunomiya Museum of Art line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Toyosatodai Entrance" and walk for 20 minutes. ; By Car * From National Route 119 (Toyosato Bypass): ** Proceed north from the Nagaokamachi Intersection along the {{ill|Nagaoka Road|ja|長岡街道}} for 3 minutes. * From {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Route 63|ja|栃木県道63号藤原宇都宮線}}: ** Head west from the Sekibori-cho North Intersection via Nagaoka Road for 3 minutes. == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Nagaoka Hyakuana}} * {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群}} * {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群}} * {{ill|Taniguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}} * {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}} * {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}} * {{ill|Hyakumoku|ja|百目鬼}} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun}} {{citation needed|Nagaoka Hyakka Kofun}} {{citation needed|Nagaoka Hundred Caves}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Rock-cut tombs]] [[Category:Ancient tombs in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Utsunomiya]] [[Category:Tochigi Prefecture-Historic Site1 historic sites]] [[Category:Utsunomiya Scenic Spots]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Nagaoka Hyakka Kofun]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagaoka Hundred Caves]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagaoka Hundred Caves]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Nagaoka Hundred Caves]] [[sn:Nagaoka Hyakka Kofun]] [[ja:%E9%95%B7%E5%B2%A1%E7%99%BE%E7%A9%B4%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[en:Nagaoka Hyakka Kofun]] [[ja:&action=edit]] {{translated page|ja|長岡百穴古墳|version=102535258|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Eastern Cluster
[[File:Nagaoka Hyakuana, nishi-gun.jpg|280px]]
Western Cluster | location = {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft}}, Nagaokachō | coordinates = {{Coord|36|35|35|N|139|53|3|E}} | type = Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster | area = 52 tombs (44 in the Eastern Cluster, 8 in the Western Cluster) | built = Early 7th century | Interred_individual = | Historic Site designation1 = Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun) | burial goods = }} '''Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun''' (長岡百穴古墳, also known as Nagaoka Hyakuana or the Nagaoka Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster) is a cluster of {{ill|rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市|12=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is Historic Site1 as a historic site by the Tochigi Prefecture. == Overview == The Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun is considered a family-oriented burial site, with tombs believed to have been constructed in the early 7th century. The cluster comprises 52 tombs, with 44 in the Eastern Cluster and 8 in the Western Cluster. Each tomb is approximately 1 meter in height and width and 2 meters in depth, carved into the sloped pumice tuff bedrock facing south. By modern times, the tombs were already exposed, with their original type lost. burial goods that could have been buried with the deceased are no longer present, making detailed research challenging. However, the tomb designs, including some with side channels on the floors, indicate advanced craftsmanship characteristic of the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}. Many of the tomb walls feature carvings of {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩|12=draft}} and {{ill|Hayagriva (Buddhism)|lt=Bato Kannon|ja|馬頭観音|12=draft}}, believed to date back to the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代|12=draft}} through the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}}. This suggests prolonged exposure to the elements and considerable cultural use, as evidenced by the inclusion of the site in local folklore, such as the {{ill|Hyakumoku Densetsu (One Hundred Eyes Legend)|ja|百目鬼伝説|12=draft}}. File:Nagaoka hyakuana inside.jpg|Interior view File:Nagaoka hyakuana monument.jpg|Poetic inscription by {{ill|Misai Kosugi|ja|小杉放庵|12=draft}} == Surrounding Environment == The Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun is situated in the northern part of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市|12=draft}}, at the southwestern foothills of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=draft}}, near {{ill|Tomatsuriyama|ja|戸祭山|12=draft}}. The site is located on a plateau flanked by the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川|12=draft}}. The surrounding area features several other tumuli and ancient sites, including the {{ill|Taniguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群|12=draft}}, and the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=draft}}, as well as remains from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|12=draft}}, such as the {{ill|Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡|12=draft}}. These sites are linked by the {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|12=draft}}. The tombs are surrounded by greenery, including {{ill|Quercus serrata|ja|コナラ|12=draft}} and {{ill|Quercus acutissima|ja|クヌギ|12=draft}}. The area is Historic Site1 as the Nagaoka Green Conservation Area by Tochigi Prefecture{{Cite web |url=https://www.pref.tochigi.lg.jp/d04/30jikannti/10nagaoka.html|title=10 Nagaoka Green Conservation Area|website=Tochigi Prefecture |accessdate=2024-11-11}}. Urbanization has increased in the surrounding area with the development of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路|12=draft}}. == Historic Site == === Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Sites ** Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun - Designated on July 26, 1955. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yomiuri Shimbun, Tochigi Edition, May 8, 2016, p. 27. == Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅|12=draft}} (JR West Exit): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車|12=draft}} to the Teikyo University/Utsunomiya Museum of Art line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Toyosatodai Entrance" and walk for 20 minutes. ; By Car * From National Route 119 (Toyosato Bypass): ** Proceed north from the Nagaokamachi Intersection along the {{ill|Nagaoka Road|ja|長岡街道|12=draft}} for 3 minutes. * From {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Route 63|ja|栃木県道63号藤原宇都宮線|12=draft}}: ** Head west from the Sekibori-cho North Intersection via Nagaoka Road for 3 minutes. == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Nagaoka Hyakuana}} * {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群|12=draft}} * {{ill|Taniguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=draft}} * {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|12=draft}} * {{ill|Hyakumoku|ja|百目鬼|12=draft}} {{citation needed|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun}} {{citation needed|Nagaoka Hyakka Kofun}} {{citation needed|Nagaoka Hundred Caves}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|長岡百穴古墳|version=102535258|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Rock-cut tombs]] [[Category:Ancient tombs in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Utsunomiya]] [[Category:Tochigi Prefecture-Historic Site1 historic sites]] [[Category:Utsunomiya Scenic Spots]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Nagaoka Hyakka Kofun]] }} [[sn:Nagaoka Hundred Caves]] [[sn:Nagaoka Hyakka Kofun]] [[ja:%E9%95%B7%E5%B2%A1%E7%99%BE%E7%A9%B4%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[en:Nagaoka Hyakka Kofun]] [[ja:&action=edit]]

Alight at "Toyosatodai Entrance" and walk for 20 minutes. ; By Car * From National Route 119 (Toyosato Bypass): ** Proceed north from the Nagaokamachi Intersection along the {{ill|Nagaoka Road|ja|長岡街道|qq=draft}} for 3 minutes. * From {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Route 63|ja|栃木県道63号藤原宇都宮線|qq=draft}}: ** Head west from the Sekibori-cho North Intersection via Nagaoka Road for 3 minutes. == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Nagaoka Hyakuana}} * {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Taniguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Hyakumoku|ja|百目鬼|qq=draft}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|長岡百穴古墳|version=102535258|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Rock-cut tombs]] [[Category:Ancient tombs in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Utsunomiya]] [[Category:Tochigi Prefecture-Historic Site1 historic sites]] [[Category:Utsunomiya Scenic Spots]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Nagaoka Hyakka Kofun]] }} [[sn:Nagaoka Hundred Caves]] [[sn:Nagaoka Hyakka Kofun]] [[ja:%E9%95%B7%E5%B2%A1%E7%99%BE%E7%A9%B4%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[en:Nagaoka Hyakka Kofun]] [[ja:&action=edit]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun | image = [[File:Nagaoka Hyakuana, higashi-gun-2.jpg|280px]]
Eastern Cluster
[[File:Nagaoka Hyakuana, nishi-gun.jpg|280px]]
Western Cluster | location = {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya|qq=}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Prefecture|qq=}}, Nagaokachō | coordinates = {{Coord|36|35|35|N|139|53|3|E}} | type = Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster | area = 52 tombs (44 in the Eastern Cluster, 8 in the Western Cluster) | built = Early 7th century | Interred_individual = | Historic Site designation1 = Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun) | burial goods = }} '''Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun''' (長岡百穴古墳, also known as Nagaoka Hyakuana or the Nagaoka Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster) is a cluster of {{ill|rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rock-cut tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya|qq=}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is Historic Site1 as a historic site by the Tochigi Prefecture. == Overview == The Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun is considered a family-oriented burial site, with tombs believed to have been constructed in the early 7th century. The cluster comprises 52 tombs, with 44 in the Eastern Cluster and 8 in the Western Cluster. Each tomb is approximately 1 meter in height and width and 2 meters in depth, carved into the sloped pumice tuff bedrock facing south. By modern times, the tombs were already exposed, with their original type lost. burial goods that could have been buried with the deceased are no longer present, making detailed research challenging. However, the tomb designs, including some with side channels on the floors, indicate advanced craftsmanship characteristic of the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun period|qq=}}. Many of the tomb walls feature carvings of {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jizō Bosatsu|qq=}} and {{ill|Hayagriva (Buddhism)|lt=Bato Kannon|ja|馬頭観音|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayagriva (Buddhism)|qq=}}, believed to date back to the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi period|qq=}} through the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}}. This suggests prolonged exposure to the elements and considerable cultural use, as evidenced by the inclusion of the site in local folklore, such as the {{ill|Hyakumoku Densetsu (One Hundred Eyes Legend)|ja|百目鬼伝説|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyakumoku Densetsu (One Hundred Eyes Legend)|qq=}}. File:Nagaoka hyakuana inside.jpg|Interior view File:Nagaoka hyakuana monument.jpg|Poetic inscription by {{ill|Misai Kosugi|ja|小杉放庵|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misai Kosugi|qq=}} == Surrounding Environment == The Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun is situated in the northern part of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya|qq=}}, at the southwestern foothills of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}}, near {{ill|Tomatsuriyama|ja|戸祭山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuriyama|qq=}}. The site is located on a plateau flanked by the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa River (Tone River System)|qq=}} and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamagawa River|qq=}}. The surrounding area features several other tumuli and ancient sites, including the {{ill|Taniguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taniguchiyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, and the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, as well as remains from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara period|qq=}}, such as the {{ill|Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|qq=}}. These sites are linked by the {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosato Mahoroba Road|qq=}}. The tombs are surrounded by greenery, including {{ill|Quercus serrata|ja|コナラ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Quercus serrata|qq=}} and {{ill|Quercus acutissima|ja|クヌギ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Quercus acutissima|qq=}}. The area is Historic Site1 as the Nagaoka Green Conservation Area by Tochigi Prefecture{{Cite web |url=https://www.pref.tochigi.lg.jp/d04/30jikannti/10nagaoka.html|title=10 Nagaoka Green Conservation Area|website=Tochigi Prefecture |accessdate=2024-11-11}}. Urbanization has increased in the surrounding area with the development of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Ring Road|qq=}}. == Historic Site == === Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Sites ** Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun - Designated on July 26, 1955. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yomiuri Shimbun, Tochigi Edition, May 8, 2016, p. 27. == Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Station|qq=}} (JR West Exit): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Bus|qq=}} to the Teikyo University/Utsunomiya Museum of Art line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Toyosatodai Entrance" and walk for 20 minutes. ; By Car * From National Route 119 (Toyosato Bypass): ** Proceed north from the Nagaokamachi Intersection along the {{ill|Nagaoka Road|ja|長岡街道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Road|qq=}} for 3 minutes. * From {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Route 63|ja|栃木県道63号藤原宇都宮線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Prefecture Route 63|qq=}}: ** Head west from the Sekibori-cho North Intersection via Nagaoka Road for 3 minutes. == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Nagaoka Hyakuana}} * {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama Kofun Cluster|qq=}} * {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}} * {{ill|Taniguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taniguchiyama Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosato Mahoroba Road|qq=}} * {{ill|Hyakumoku|ja|百目鬼|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyakumoku|qq=}} So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun | image = [[File:Nagaoka Hyakuana, higashi-gun-2.jpg|280px]]
Eastern Cluster
[[File:Nagaoka Hyakuana, nishi-gun.jpg|280px]]
Western Cluster | location = {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya|qq=}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Prefecture|qq=}}, Nagaokachō | coordinates = {{Coord|36|35|35|N|139|53|3|E}} | type = Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster | area = 52 tombs (44 in the Eastern Cluster, 8 in the Western Cluster) | built = Early 7th century | Interred_individual = | Historic Site designation1 = Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun) | burial goods = }} '''Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun''' (長岡百穴古墳, also known as Nagaoka Hyakuana or the Nagaoka Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster) is a cluster of {{ill|rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rock-cut tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya|qq=}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is Historic Site1 as a historic site by the Tochigi Prefecture. == Overview == The Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun is considered a family-oriented burial site, with tombs believed to have been constructed in the early 7th century. The cluster comprises 52 tombs, with 44 in the Eastern Cluster and 8 in the Western Cluster. Each tomb is approximately 1 meter in height and width and 2 meters in depth, carved into the sloped pumice tuff bedrock facing south. By modern times, the tombs were already exposed, with their original type lost. burial goods that could have been buried with the deceased are no longer present, making detailed research challenging. However, the tomb designs, including some with side channels on the floors, indicate advanced craftsmanship characteristic of the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun period|qq=}}. Many of the tomb walls feature carvings of {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jizō Bosatsu|qq=}} and {{ill|Hayagriva (Buddhism)|lt=Bato Kannon|ja|馬頭観音|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayagriva (Buddhism)|qq=}}, believed to date back to the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi period|qq=}} through the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}}. This suggests prolonged exposure to the elements and considerable cultural use, as evidenced by the inclusion of the site in local folklore, such as the {{ill|Hyakumoku Densetsu (One Hundred Eyes Legend)|ja|百目鬼伝説|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyakumoku Densetsu (One Hundred Eyes Legend)|qq=}}. File:Nagaoka hyakuana inside.jpg|Interior view File:Nagaoka hyakuana monument.jpg|Poetic inscription by {{ill|Misai Kosugi|ja|小杉放庵|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misai Kosugi|qq=}} == Surrounding Environment == The Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun is situated in the northern part of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya|qq=}}, at the southwestern foothills of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}}, near {{ill|Tomatsuriyama|ja|戸祭山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuriyama|qq=}}. The site is located on a plateau flanked by the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa River (Tone River System)|qq=}} and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamagawa River|qq=}}. The surrounding area features several other tumuli and ancient sites, including the {{ill|Taniguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taniguchiyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, and the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, as well as remains from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara period|qq=}}, such as the {{ill|Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|qq=}}. These sites are linked by the {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosato Mahoroba Road|qq=}}. The tombs are surrounded by greenery, including {{ill|Quercus serrata|ja|コナラ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Quercus serrata|qq=}} and {{ill|Quercus acutissima|ja|クヌギ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Quercus acutissima|qq=}}. The area is Historic Site1 as the Nagaoka Green Conservation Area by Tochigi Prefecture{{Cite web |url=https://www.pref.tochigi.lg.jp/d04/30jikannti/10nagaoka.html|title=10 Nagaoka Green Conservation Area|website=Tochigi Prefecture |accessdate=2024-11-11}}. Urbanization has increased in the surrounding area with the development of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Ring Road|qq=}}. == Historic Site == === Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Sites ** Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun - Designated on July 26, 1955. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yomiuri Shimbun, Tochigi Edition, May 8, 2016, p. 27. == Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Station|qq=}} (JR West Exit): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Bus|qq=}} to the Teikyo University/Utsunomiya Museum of Art line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Toyosatodai Entrance" and walk for 20 minutes. ; By Car * From National Route 119 (Toyosato Bypass): ** Proceed north from the Nagaokamachi Intersection along the {{ill|Nagaoka Road|ja|長岡街道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Road|qq=}} for 3 minutes. * From {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Route 63|ja|栃木県道63号藤原宇都宮線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Prefecture Route 63|qq=}}: ** Head west from the Sekibori-cho North Intersection via Nagaoka Road for 3 minutes. == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Nagaoka Hyakuana}} * {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama Kofun Cluster|qq=}} * {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}} * {{ill|Taniguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taniguchiyama Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosato Mahoroba Road|qq=}} * {{ill|Dōmeki|ja|百目鬼|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyakumoku|qq=}} [[File:Nagaoka Hyakuana, nishi-gun.jpg|280px]]
Western Cluster | location = {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya|qq=}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Prefecture|qq=}}, Nagaokachō | coordinates = {{Coord|36|35|35|N|139|53|3|E}} | type = Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster | area = 52 tombs (44 in the Eastern Cluster, 8 in the Western Cluster) | built = Early 7th century | Interred_individual = | Historic Site designation1 = Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun) | burial goods = }} '''Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun''' (長岡百穴古墳, also known as Nagaoka Hyakuana or the Nagaoka Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster) is a cluster of {{ill|rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rock-cut tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya|qq=}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is Historic Site1 as a historic site by the Tochigi Prefecture. == Overview == The Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun is considered a family-oriented burial site, with tombs believed to have been constructed in the early 7th century. The cluster comprises 52 tombs, with 44 in the Eastern Cluster and 8 in the Western Cluster. Each tomb is approximately 1 meter in height and width and 2 meters in depth, carved into the sloped pumice tuff bedrock facing south. By modern times, the tombs were already exposed, with their original type lost. burial goods that could have been buried with the deceased are no longer present, making detailed research challenging. However, the tomb designs, including some with side channels on the floors, indicate advanced craftsmanship characteristic of the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun period|qq=}}. Many of the tomb walls feature carvings of {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jizō Bosatsu|qq=}} and {{ill|Hayagriva (Buddhism)|lt=Bato Kannon|ja|馬頭観音|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayagriva (Buddhism)|qq=}}, believed to date back to the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi period|qq=}} through the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}}. This suggests prolonged exposure to the elements and considerable cultural use, as evidenced by the inclusion of the site in local folklore, such as the {{ill|Hyakumoku Densetsu (One Hundred Eyes Legend)|ja|百目鬼伝説|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyakumoku Densetsu (One Hundred Eyes Legend)|qq=}}. File:Nagaoka hyakuana inside.jpg|Interior view File:Nagaoka hyakuana monument.jpg|Poetic inscription by {{ill|Misai Kosugi|ja|小杉放庵|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misai Kosugi|qq=}} == Surrounding Environment == The Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun is situated in the northern part of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya|qq=}}, at the southwestern foothills of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}}, near {{ill|Tomatsuriyama|ja|戸祭山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuriyama|qq=}}. The site is located on a plateau flanked by the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa River (Tone River System)|qq=}} and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamagawa River|qq=}}. The surrounding area features several other tumuli and ancient sites, including the {{ill|Taniguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taniguchiyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, and the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, as well as remains from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara period|qq=}}, such as the {{ill|Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|qq=}}. These sites are linked by the {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosato Mahoroba Road|qq=}}. The tombs are surrounded by greenery, including {{ill|Quercus serrata|ja|コナラ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Quercus serrata|qq=}} and {{ill|Quercus acutissima|ja|クヌギ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Quercus acutissima|qq=}}. The area is Historic Site1 as the Nagaoka Green Conservation Area by Tochigi Prefecture{{Cite web |url=https://www.pref.tochigi.lg.jp/d04/30jikannti/10nagaoka.html|title=10 Nagaoka Green Conservation Area|website=Tochigi Prefecture |accessdate=2024-11-11}}. Urbanization has increased in the surrounding area with the development of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Ring Road|qq=}}. == Historic Site == === Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Sites ** Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun - Designated on July 26, 1955. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yomiuri Shimbun, Tochigi Edition, May 8, 2016, p. 27. == Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Station|qq=}} (JR West Exit): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Bus|qq=}} to the Teikyo University/Utsunomiya Museum of Art line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Toyosatodai Entrance" and walk for 20 minutes. ; By Car * From National Route 119 (Toyosato Bypass): ** Proceed north from the Nagaokamachi Intersection along the {{ill|Nagaoka Road|ja|長岡街道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Road|qq=}} for 3 minutes. * From {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Route 63|ja|栃木県道63号藤原宇都宮線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Prefecture Many of the tomb walls feature carvings of {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jizō Bosatsu|qq=}} and {{ill|Hayagriva (Buddhism)|lt=Bato Kannon|ja|馬頭観音|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayagriva (Buddhism)|qq=}}, believed to date back to the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi period|qq=}} through the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}}. This suggests prolonged exposure to the elements and considerable cultural use, as evidenced by the inclusion of the site in local folklore, such as the {{ill|Hyakumoku Densetsu (One Hundred Eyes Legend)|ja|百目鬼伝説|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyakumoku Densetsu (One Hundred Eyes Legend)|qq=}}. File:Nagaoka hyakuana inside.jpg|Interior view File:Nagaoka hyakuana monument.jpg|Poetic inscription by {{ill|Misai Kosugi|ja|小杉放庵|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misai Kosugi|qq=}} == Surrounding Environment == The Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun is situated in the northern part of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya|qq=}}, at the southwestern foothills of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}}, near {{ill|Tomatsuriyama|ja|戸祭山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuriyama|qq=}}. The site is located on a plateau flanked by the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa River (Tone River System)|qq=}} and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamagawa River|qq=}}. The surrounding area features several other tumuli and ancient sites, including the {{ill|Taniguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taniguchiyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, and the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, as well as remains from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara period|qq=}}, such as the {{ill|Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|qq=}}. These sites are linked by the {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosato Mahoroba Road|qq=}}. The tombs are surrounded by greenery, including {{ill|Quercus serrata|ja|コナラ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Quercus serrata|qq=}} and {{ill|Quercus acutissima|ja|クヌギ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Quercus acutissima|qq=}}. The area is Historic Site1 as the Nagaoka Green Conservation Area by Tochigi Prefecture{{Cite web |url=https://www.pref.tochigi.lg.jp/d04/30jikannti/10nagaoka.html|title=10 Nagaoka Green Conservation Area|website=Tochigi Prefecture |accessdate=2024-11-11}}. Urbanization has increased in the surrounding area with the development of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Ring Road|qq=}}. == Historic Site == === Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Sites ** Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun - Designated on July 26, 1955. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yomiuri Shimbun, Tochigi Edition, May 8, 2016, p. 27. == Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Station|qq=}} (JR West Exit): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Bus|qq=}} to the Teikyo University/Utsunomiya Museum of Art line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Toyosatodai Entrance" and walk for 20 minutes. ; By Car * From National Route 119 (Toyosato Bypass): ** Proceed north from the Nagaokamachi Intersection along the {{ill|Nagaoka Road|ja|長岡街道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Road|qq=}} for 3 minutes. * From {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Route 63|ja|栃木県道63号藤原宇都宮線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Prefecture Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}} * {{ill|Taniguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taniguchiyama Kofun|qq=}} * Eastern Cluster
[[File:Nagaoka Hyakuana, nishi-gun.jpg|280px]]
Western Cluster | location = {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya|qq=}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Prefecture|qq=}}, Nagaokachō | coordinates = {{Coord|36|35|35|N|139|53|3|E}} | type = Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster | area = 52 tombs (44 in the Eastern Cluster, 8 in the Western Cluster) | built = Early 7th century | Interred_individual = | Historic Site designation1 = Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun) | burial goods = }} '''Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun''' (長岡百穴古墳, also known as Nagaoka Hyakuana or the Nagaoka Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster) is a cluster of {{ill|rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rock-cut tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya|qq=}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is Historic Site1 as a historic site by the Tochigi Prefecture. == Overview == The Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun is considered a family-oriented burial site, with tombs believed to have been constructed in the early 7th century. The cluster comprises 52 tombs, with 44 in the Eastern Cluster and 8 in the Western Cluster. Each tomb is approximately 1 meter in height and width and 2 meters in depth, carved into the sloped pumice tuff bedrock facing south. By modern times, the tombs were already exposed, with their original type lost. burial goods that could have been buried with the deceased are no longer present, making detailed research challenging. However, the tomb designs, including some with side channels on the floors, indicate advanced craftsmanship characteristic of the {{ill|Late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun period|qq=}}. Many of the tomb walls feature carvings of {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jizō Bosatsu|qq=}} and {{ill|Hayagriva (Buddhism)|lt=Bato Kannon|ja|馬頭観音|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayagriva (Buddhism)|qq=}}, believed to date back to the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi period|qq=}} through the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}}. This suggests prolonged exposure to the elements and considerable cultural use, as evidenced by the inclusion of the site in local folklore, such as the {{ill|Hyakumoku Densetsu (One Hundred Eyes Legend)|ja|百目鬼伝説|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyakumoku Densetsu (One Hundred Eyes Legend)|qq=}}. File:Nagaoka hyakuana inside.jpg|Interior view File:Nagaoka hyakuana monument.jpg|Poetic inscription by {{ill|Misai Kosugi|ja|小杉放庵|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misai Kosugi|qq=}} == Surrounding Environment == The Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun is situated in the northern part of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya|qq=}}, at the southwestern foothills of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}}, near {{ill|Tomatsuriyama|ja|戸祭山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuriyama|qq=}}. The site is located on a plateau flanked by the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa River (Tone River System)|qq=}} and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamagawa River|qq=}}. The surrounding area features several other tumuli and ancient sites, including the {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|lt=Yaguchiyama Kofun|lt=Taniguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taniguchiyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, and the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, as well as remains from the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara period|qq=}}, such as the {{ill|Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|qq=}}. These sites are linked by the {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosato Mahoroba Road|qq=}}. The tombs are surrounded by greenery, including {{ill|Quercus serrata|ja|コナラ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Quercus serrata|qq=}} and {{ill|Quercus acutissima|ja|クヌギ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Quercus acutissima|qq=}}. The area is Historic Site1 as the Nagaoka Green Conservation Area by Tochigi Prefecture{{Cite web |url=https://www.pref.tochigi.lg.jp/d04/30jikannti/10nagaoka.html|title=10 Nagaoka Green Conservation Area|website=Tochigi Prefecture |accessdate=2024-11-11}}. Urbanization has increased in the surrounding area with the development of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Ring Road|qq=}}. == Historic Site == === Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Sites ** Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun - Designated on July 26, 1955. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yomiuri Shimbun, Tochigi Edition, May 8, 2016, p. 27. == Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Station|qq=}} (JR West Exit): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Bus|qq=}} to the Teikyo University/Utsunomiya Museum of Art line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Toyosatodai Entrance" and walk for 20 minutes. ; By Car * From National Route 119 (Toyosato Bypass): ** Proceed north from the Nagaokamachi Intersection along the {{ill|Nagaoka Road|ja|長岡街道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Road|qq=}} for 3 minutes. * From {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Route 63|ja|栃木県道63号藤原宇都宮線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Prefecture Western Cluster | location = [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], Nagaokachō | coordinates = {{Coord|36|35|35|N|139|53|3|E}} | type = Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster | area = 52 tombs (44 in the Eastern Cluster, 8 in the Western Cluster) | built = Early 7th century | Interred_individual = | Historic Site designation1 = Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun) | burial goods = }} '''Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun''' (長岡百穴古墳, also known as Nagaoka Hyakuana or the Nagaoka Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster) is a cluster of {{ill|rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rock-cut tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} located in [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]], Tochigi Prefecture. It is Historic Site1 as a historic site by the Tochigi Prefecture. == Overview == The Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun is considered a family-oriented burial site, with tombs believed to have been constructed in the early 7th century. The cluster comprises 52 tombs, with 44 in the Eastern Cluster and 8 in the Western Cluster. Each tomb is approximately 1 meter in height and width and 2 meters in depth, carved into the sloped pumice tuff bedrock facing south. By modern times, the tombs were already exposed, with their original type lost. burial goods that could have been buried with the deceased are no longer present, making detailed research challenging. However, the tomb designs, including some with side channels on the floors, indicate advanced craftsmanship characteristic of the [[:en:Kofun period|Late Kofun period]]. Many of the tomb walls feature carvings of [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]] and [[:en:Hayagriva (Buddhism)|Bato Kannon]], believed to date back to the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] through the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. This suggests prolonged exposure to the elements and considerable cultural use, as evidenced by the inclusion of the site in local folklore, such as the {{ill|Hyakumoku Densetsu (One Hundred Eyes Legend)|ja|百目鬼伝説|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyakumoku Densetsu (One Hundred Eyes Legend)|qq=}}. File:Nagaoka hyakuana inside.jpg|Interior view File:Nagaoka hyakuana monument.jpg|Poetic inscription by [[:en:Misai Kosugi|Misai Kosugi]] == Surrounding Environment == The Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun is situated in the northern part of [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]], at the southwestern foothills of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}}, near {{ill|Tomatsuriyama|ja|戸祭山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuriyama|qq=}}. The site is located on a plateau flanked by the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa River (Tone River System)|qq=}} and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamagawa River|qq=}}. The surrounding area features several other tumuli and ancient sites, including the {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|lt=Yaguchiyama Kofun|lt=Taniguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taniguchiyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, and the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, as well as remains from the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], such as the {{ill|Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|qq=}}. These sites are linked by the {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosato Mahoroba Road|qq=}}. The tombs are surrounded by greenery, including [[:en:Quercus serrata|Quercus serrata]] and [[:en:Quercus acutissima|Quercus acutissima]]. The area is Historic Site1 as the Nagaoka Green Conservation Area by Tochigi Prefecture{{Cite web |url=https://www.pref.tochigi.lg.jp/d04/30jikannti/10nagaoka.html|title=10 Nagaoka Green Conservation Area|website=Tochigi Prefecture |accessdate=2024-11-11}}. Urbanization has increased in the surrounding area with the development of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Ring Road|qq=}}. == Historic Site == === Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Sites ** Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun - Designated on July 26, 1955. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yomiuri Shimbun, Tochigi Edition, May 8, 2016, p. 27. == Access == ; By Bus * From [[:en:Utsunomiya Station|Utsunomiya Station]] (JR West Exit): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Bus|qq=}} to the Teikyo University/Utsunomiya Museum of Art line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Toyosatodai Entrance" and walk for 20 minutes. ; By Car * From National Route 119 (Toyosato Bypass): ** Proceed north from the Nagaokamachi Intersection along the {{ill|Nagaoka Road|ja|長岡街道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Road|qq=}} for 3 minutes. * From {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Route 63|ja|栃木県道63号藤原宇都宮線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Prefecture revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:うつのみや百景
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun | image = [[File:Nagaoka Hyakuana, higashi-gun-2.jpg|280px]]
Eastern Cluster
[[File:Nagaoka Hyakuana, nishi-gun.jpg|280px]]
Western Cluster | location = [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], Nagaokachō | coordinates = {{Coord|36|35|35|N|139|53|3|E}} | type = Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster | area = 52 tombs (44 in the Eastern Cluster, 8 in the Western Cluster) | built = Early 7th century | Interred_individual = | Historic Site designation1 = Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun) | burial goods = }} '''Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun''' (長岡百穴古墳, also known as Nagaoka Hyakuana or the Nagaoka Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster) is a cluster of {{ill|rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rock-cut tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} located in [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]], Tochigi Prefecture. It is Historic Site1 as a historic site by the Tochigi Prefecture. == Overview == The Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun is considered a family-oriented burial site, with tombs believed to have been constructed in the early 7th century. The cluster comprises 52 tombs, with 44 in the Eastern Cluster and 8 in the Western Cluster. Each tomb is approximately 1 meter in height and width and 2 meters in depth, carved into the sloped pumice tuff bedrock facing south. By modern times, the tombs were already exposed, with their original type lost. burial goods that could have been buried with the deceased are no longer present, making detailed research challenging. However, the tomb designs, including some with side channels on the floors, indicate advanced craftsmanship characteristic of the [[:en:Kofun period|Late Kofun period]]. Many of the tomb walls feature carvings of [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]] and [[:en:Hayagriva (Buddhism)|Bato Kannon]], believed to date back to the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] through the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. This suggests prolonged exposure to the elements and considerable cultural use, as evidenced by the inclusion of the site in local folklore, such as the {{ill|Hyakumoku Densetsu (One Hundred Eyes Legend)|ja|百目鬼伝説|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyakumoku Densetsu (One Hundred Eyes Legend)|qq=}}. File:Nagaoka hyakuana inside.jpg|Interior view File:Nagaoka hyakuana monument.jpg|Poetic inscription by [[:en:Misai Kosugi|Misai Kosugi]] == Surrounding Environment == The Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun is situated in the northern part of [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]], at the southwestern foothills of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}}, near {{ill|Tomatsuriyama|ja|戸祭山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuriyama|qq=}}. The site is located on a plateau flanked by the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa River (Tone River System)|qq=}} and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamagawa River|qq=}}. The surrounding area features several other tumuli and ancient sites, including the {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|lt=Yaguchiyama Kofun|lt=Taniguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taniguchiyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, and the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, as well as remains from the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], such as the {{ill|Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|qq=}}. These sites are linked by the {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosato Mahoroba Road|qq=}}. The tombs are surrounded by greenery, including [[:en:Quercus serrata|Quercus serrata]] and [[:en:Quercus acutissima|Quercus acutissima]]. The area is Historic Site1 as the Nagaoka Green Conservation Area by Tochigi Prefecture{{Cite web |url=https://www.pref.tochigi.lg.jp/d04/30jikannti/10nagaoka.html|title=10 Nagaoka Green Conservation Area|website=Tochigi Prefecture |accessdate=2024-11-11}}. Urbanization has increased in the surrounding area with the development of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Ring Road|qq=}}. == Historic Site == === Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Sites ** Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun - Designated on July 26, 1955. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yomiuri Shimbun, Tochigi Edition, May 8, 2016, p. 27. == Access == ; By Bus * From [[:en:Utsunomiya Station|Utsunomiya Station]] (JR West Exit): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Bus|qq=}} to the Teikyo University/Utsunomiya Museum of Art line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Toyosatodai Entrance" and walk for 20 minutes. ; By Car * From National Route 119 (Toyosato Bypass): ** Proceed north from the Nagaokamachi Intersection along the {{ill|Nagaoka Road|ja|長岡街道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Road|qq=}} for 3 minutes. * From {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Route 63|ja|栃木県道63号藤原宇都宮線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Prefecture Route 63|qq=}}: ** Head west from the Sekibori-cho North Intersection via Nagaoka Road for 3 minutes. == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Nagaoka Hyakuana}} * {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama Kofun Cluster|qq=}} * {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}} * {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|lt=Yaguchiyama Kofun|lt=Taniguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taniguchiyama Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosato Mahoroba Road|qq=}} * {{ill|Dōmeki|ja|百目鬼|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyakumoku|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} Western Cluster | location = [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], Nagaokachō | coordinates = {{Coord|36|35|35|N|139|53|3|E}} | type = Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster | area = 52 tombs (44 in the Eastern Cluster, 8 in the Western Cluster) | built = Early 7th century | Interred_individual = | Historic Site designation1 = Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun) | burial goods = }} '''Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun''' (長岡百穴古墳, also known as Nagaoka Hyakuana or the Nagaoka Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster) is a cluster of {{ill|rock-cut tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rock-cut tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} located in [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]], Tochigi Prefecture. It is Historic Site1 as a historic site by the Tochigi Prefecture. == Overview == The Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun is considered a family-oriented burial site, with tombs believed to have been constructed in the early 7th century. The cluster comprises 52 tombs, with 44 in the Eastern Cluster and 8 in the Western Cluster. Each tomb is approximately 1 meter in height and width and 2 meters in depth, carved into the sloped pumice tuff bedrock facing south. By modern times, the tombs were already exposed, with their original type lost. burial goods that could have been buried with the deceased are no longer present, making detailed research challenging. However, the tomb designs, including some with side channels on the floors, indicate advanced craftsmanship characteristic of the [[:en:Kofun period|Late Kofun period]]. Many of the tomb walls feature carvings of [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]] and [[:en:Hayagriva (Buddhism)|Bato Kannon]], believed to date back to the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] through the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. This suggests prolonged exposure to the elements and considerable cultural use, as evidenced by the inclusion of the site in local folklore, such as the {{ill|Hyakumoku Densetsu (One Hundred Eyes Legend)|ja|百目鬼伝説|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyakumoku Densetsu (One Hundred Eyes Legend)|qq=}}. File:Nagaoka hyakuana inside.jpg|Interior view File:Nagaoka hyakuana monument.jpg|Poetic inscription by [[:en:Misai Kosugi|Misai Kosugi]] == Surrounding Environment == The Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun is situated in the northern part of [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]], at the southwestern foothills of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}}, near {{ill|Tomatsuriyama|ja|戸祭山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuriyama|qq=}}. The site is located on a plateau flanked by the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa River (Tone River System)|qq=}} and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamagawa River|qq=}}. The surrounding area features several other tumuli and ancient sites, including the {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|lt=Yaguchiyama Kofun|lt=Taniguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taniguchiyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, and the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, as well as remains from the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], such as the {{ill|Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|qq=}}. These sites are linked by the {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosato Mahoroba Road|qq=}}. The tombs are surrounded by greenery, including [[:en:Quercus serrata|Quercus serrata]] and [[:en:Quercus acutissima|Quercus acutissima]]. The area is Historic Site1 as the Nagaoka Green Conservation Area by Tochigi Prefecture{{Cite web |url=https://www.pref.tochigi.lg.jp/d04/30jikannti/10nagaoka.html|title=10 Nagaoka Green Conservation Area|website=Tochigi Prefecture |accessdate=2024-11-11}}. Urbanization has increased in the surrounding area with the development of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Ring Road|qq=}}. == Historic Site == === Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Sites ** Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun - Designated on July 26, 1955. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yomiuri Shimbun, Tochigi Edition, May 8, 2016, p. 27. == Access == ; By Bus * From [[:en:Utsunomiya Station|Utsunomiya Station]] (JR West Exit): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Bus|qq=}} to the Teikyo University/Utsunomiya Museum of Art line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Toyosatodai Entrance" and walk for 20 minutes. ; By Car * From National Route 119 (Toyosato Bypass): ** Proceed north from the Nagaokamachi Intersection along the {{ill|Nagaoka Road|ja|長岡街道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Road|qq=}} for 3 minutes. * From {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Route 63|ja|栃木県道63号藤原宇都宮線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Prefecture Route 63|qq=}}: ** Head west from the Sekibori-cho North Intersection via Nagaoka Road for 3 minutes. == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Nagaoka Hyakuana}} * {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama Kofun Cluster|qq=}} * {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}} * {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|lt=Yaguchiyama Kofun|lt=Taniguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taniguchiyama Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosato Mahoroba Road|qq=}} * {{ill|Dōmeki|ja|百目鬼|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyakumoku|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} Road|ja|長岡街道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Road|qq=}} for 3 minutes. * From {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Route 63|ja|栃木県道63号藤原宇都宮線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Prefecture
