revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun | image = [[File:Nagaoka Hyakuana, higashi-gun-2.jpg|280px]]
Eastern Cluster
[[File:Nagaoka Hyakuana, nishi-gun.jpg|280px]]
Western Cluster | location = {{ill|Utsunomiya|en|Utsunomiya|qid=Q200589|lt=Utsunomiya|1=Utsunomiya}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|qid=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}, Nagaokachō | coordinates = {{Coord|36.593056|N|139.884167|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster | area = 52 tombs (44 in the Eastern Cluster, 8 in the Western Cluster) | built = Early 7th century | Interred_individual = | Historic Site designation1 = Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun) | burial goods = }} '''Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun''' (長岡百穴古墳, also known as Nagaoka Hyakuana or the Nagaoka Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster) is a cluster of {{ill|rock-cut tombs|lt=Yokoanabos|ja|横穴墓|1=Yokoanabo|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rock-cut tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo|qq=}} located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|en|Utsunomiya|qid=Q200589|lt=Utsunomiya|1=Utsunomiya}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is Historic Site1 as a historic site by the Tochigi Prefecture. == Overview == The Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun is considered a family-oriented burial site, with tombs believed to have been constructed in the early 7th century. The cluster comprises 52 tombs, with 44 in the Eastern Cluster and 8 in the Western Cluster. Each tomb is approximately 1 meter in height and width and 2 meters in depth, carved into the sloped pumice tuff bedrock facing south. By modern times, the tombs were already exposed, with their original type lost. burial goods that could have been buried with the deceased are no longer present, making detailed research challenging. However, the tomb designs, including some with side channels on the floors, indicate advanced craftsmanship characteristic of the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Late Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}. Many of the tomb walls feature carvings of {{ill|Kṣitigarbha|en|Kṣitigarbha|qid=Q604687|lt=Jizō Bosatsu|1=Kṣitigarbha}} and {{ill|Hayagriva (Buddhism)|en|Hayagriva (Buddhism)|qid=Q17004306|lt=Bato Kannon|1=Hayagriva (Buddhism)}}, believed to date back to the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|qid=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}} through the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}. This suggests prolonged exposure to the elements and considerable cultural use, as evidenced by the inclusion of the site in local folklore, such as the {{ill|Hyakumoku Densetsu (One Hundred Eyes Legend)|ja|百目鬼伝説|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyakumoku Densetsu (One Hundred Eyes Legend)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. File:Nagaoka hyakuana inside.jpg|Interior view File:Nagaoka hyakuana monument.jpg|Poetic inscription by {{ill|Misai Kosugi|en|Misai Kosugi|qid=Q11460982|lt=Misai Kosugi|1=Misai Kosugi}} == Surrounding Environment == The Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun is situated in the northern part of {{ill|Utsunomiya|en|Utsunomiya|qid=Q200589|lt=Utsunomiya|1=Utsunomiya}}, at the southwestern foothills of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}}, near {{ill|Tomatsuriyama|ja|戸祭山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuriyama|1=Tomatsuri-yama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11496593}}. The site is located on a plateau flanked by the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa River (Tone River System)|qq=}} and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamagawa River|1=Kama River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11648095}}. The surrounding area features several other tumuli and ancient sites, including the {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|lt=Yaguchiyama Kofun|lt=Taniguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taniguchiyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135490863|1=Kitayama Kofun Cluster}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, and the {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, as well as remains from the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|qid=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}}, such as the {{ill|Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja|水道山瓦窯跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suidōyama Tile Kiln Site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. These sites are linked by the {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The tombs are surrounded by greenery, including {{ill|Quercus serrata|en|Quercus serrata|qid=Q847209|lt=Quercus serrata|1=Quercus serrata}} and {{ill|Quercus acutissima|en|Quercus acutissima|qid=Q1034859|lt=Quercus acutissima|1=Quercus acutissima}}. The area is Historic Site1 as the Nagaoka Green Conservation Area by Tochigi Prefecture{{Cite web |url=https://www.pref.tochigi.lg.jp/d04/30jikannti/10nagaoka.html|title=10 Nagaoka Green Conservation Area|website=Tochigi Prefecture |accessdate=2024-11-11}}. Urbanization has increased in the surrounding area with the development of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Ring Road|1=Utsunomiya Ring Road|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11449792}}. == Historic Site == === Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Sites ** Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun - Designated on July 26, 1955. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yomiuri Shimbun, Tochigi Edition, May 8, 2016, p. 27. == Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|en|Utsunomiya Station|qid=Q801549|lt=Utsunomiya Station|1=Utsunomiya Station}} (JR West Exit): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Bus|1=Q11655984|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11655984}} to the Teikyo University/Utsunomiya Museum of Art line (approx. 15 minutes). Alight at "Toyosatodai Entrance" and walk for 20 minutes. ; By Car * From National Route 119 (Toyosato Bypass): ** Proceed north from the Nagaokamachi Intersection along the {{ill|Nagaoka Road|ja|長岡街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Road|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} for 3 minutes. * From {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Route 63|ja|栃木県道63号藤原宇都宮線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Prefecture Route 63|1=Tochigi Prefectural Road Route 63|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11535545}}: ** Head west from the Sekibori-cho North Intersection via Nagaoka Road for 3 minutes. == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Nagaoka Hyakuana}} * {{ill|Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja|北山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitayama Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135490863|1=Kitayama Kofun Cluster}} * {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Group|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|ja|瓦塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawarazuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}} * {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|lt=Yaguchiyama Kofun|lt=Taniguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taniguchiyama Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Dōmeki|ja|百目鬼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyakumoku|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|長岡百穴古墳|version=102535258|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Nagaoka Hyakuana}} {{wikidata link|Q11652134|co|commons:Category:Nagaoka Hyakuana|ja|長岡百穴古墳}} [[Category:100 Views of Utsunomiya]] [[Category:Corridor-type kofun]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Utsunomiya]] [[Category:Kofun of Tochigi prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
